title: Core /v1/agent + MCP + skills + usage surface skips session touch and token expiry — revoked bearers keep working
detail: All ~65 handlers in app.py use static FacadeAuthenticator.authenticate_request (HMAC-only); verify_with_touch is never called there, and verify_identity_token never checks the exp claim even though CLAIM_EXPIRES_AT exists in the shared contract (no exp reference anywhere in facade src). Logout/session-revoke invalidates the touch cache and sessions row, but conversations, runs, run streams, MCP servers, skills, usage, and budgets never consult it — a revoked or logged-out bearer keeps working there indefinitely. The auth.py docstring claim that every facade route uses verify_with_touch is false.
suggestion: Route app.py handlers through verify_with_touch (client is already app-scoped), enforce exp in verify_identity_token, and add a regression test that a revoked session 401s on /v1/agent/runs.

title: Duplicate /v1/admin/adapter_registry/* registration silently shadows the stronger-auth handlers
detail: adapter_registry_routes.py:79-131 and adapter_review_routes.py:56-93 both register the same three admin routes; register_adapter_registry_routes runs first (app.py:132-133) so FastAPI registration-order matching makes the review module's handlers unreachable — and the dead trio is the one using verify_with_touch while the live trio uses static auth. test_adapter_review_routes.py passes against the wrong handlers because both produce identical upstream calls.
suggestion: Delete the admin trio from adapter_registry_routes, keep the touch-authenticated review module, and add a duplicate-path guard test over app.routes.

title: ~20 copies of _raise_for_upstream, ~25 settings_for, 9 SSE proxy closures, 3 forwarding kernels
detail: Every route module carries private copies of the same helpers (_raise_for_upstream x20, _upstream_error_detail x12, settings_for/_settings_for x~25, _safe_json + _coerce_object_or_raise x~8) plus nine identical SSE pass-through closures and three parallel JSON-forwarding kernels (app.py forward_json, me_routes' three _forward_* variants, per-module inline bodies). Several copies self-justify as avoiding circular imports; the real fix is a leaf proxy module. Roughly 2.5-4k of the 9.8k source LOC is this boilerplate, and any cross-cutting change (timeout, retry, header) is a 30-file diff.
suggestion: Extract one backend_facade/proxy.py (forward_json, forward_sse, error mapping, settings accessor) and migrate modules to it.

title: atlas_pk_ API keys are rejected (401) on the entire agent/runs surface
detail: authenticate_request explicitly raises 401 for atlas_pk_ bearers (auth.py:207-211); only verify_with_touch handles them via the backend verify endpoint, and app.py never calls it. Personal/workspace API keys minted at /v1/me/api-keys — whose stated use case is CI bots — cannot create conversations or runs, the primary automation surface.
suggestion: Same fix as the auth-tier unification; add an API-key smoke test against /v1/agent/runs.

title: docs/reference/api-surface.md documents non-existent routes and omits ~2/3 of the live surface
detail: The doc lists /v1/api-keys*, /v1/me/sessions*, /v1/workspace/providers, /v1/auth/password-reset/* — none of which exist (real paths: /v1/me/api-keys, /v1/auth/sessions, /v1/auth/password/reset/*) — and omits entire live families (tools, connectors+webhooks, inbox, home, library, memory, palette, projects, routines, team, todos, agents, adapter-registry, local-models, SIWE, MFA, wallet page): ~280 registered decorators vs ~90 documented rows. The service CLAUDE.md declares these docs the source of truth, so the divergence actively misleads.
suggestion: Regenerate the table from app.openapi() and add a CI drift check.

title: app.py is a 1,505-line monolith of identical 10-line forward handlers
detail: Bootstrap plus ~85 handlers each repeating authenticate -> forward_json(target, scoped_params); adapter_registry_routes even does a function-local import of forward_json from app to dodge the resulting circular import. A declarative route table (method, path, target, param/body policy) driving one generic handler would collapse most of the file and make the auth-tier fix a one-line policy change.
suggestion: Split agent/MCP/usage groups into modules sharing the extracted proxy kernel; keep genuinely special handlers (skills merge, SSE, telemetry relay) hand-written.

title: Root CLAUDE.md claims backend /internal/v1/* is consumed only by ai-backend — the facade consumes ~50 internal endpoints
detail: The facade proxies to /internal/v1/auth/*, /internal/v1/{me,workspace,policies,liveness,adapter_registry,audit} on backend and /internal/v1/{skills/system,audit/list} on ai-backend. The documented boundary rule no longer matches the architecture, which matters for the compliance-review audience the repo optimizes for.
suggestion: Update the doc: internal plane consumed by ai-backend AND backend-facade, exposed by neither.

title: MFA factor management duplicated across /v1/auth/mfa/* and /v1/me/mfa/* with the former apparently consumer-less
detail: auth_routes.py:620-803 exposes factors list/enroll/confirm/delete/webauthn-register against /internal/v1/auth/mfa/*, while me_routes.py:109-210 exposes the same management operations against /internal/v1/me/mfa/*. The frontend manages factors only via /v1/me/mfa/* and uses /v1/auth/mfa/* solely for challenge/verify/recovery, leaving the auth_routes factor-management routes without a known consumer.
suggestion: Deprecate the /v1/auth/mfa/factors* management routes, keeping only the login-dance trio.

title: Docker image runs Python 3.14 against the repo's 3.13-everywhere convention
detail: Dockerfile builds FROM python:3.14-slim-bookworm while pyproject pins requires-python >=3.13 and local .venvs/CI run 3.13 — the shipped container executes a different interpreter major than anything tested.
suggestion: Pin the image to python:3.13-slim or bump the convention deliberately across services.

title: Legacy /v1/session route and stale .env.example dev vars have no consumers
detail: GET /v1/session (app.py:224) has no consumer in apps/packages/tools — the frontend moved to /v1/auth/session (kept 'for one release' per comment). .env.example documents FACADE_DEV_ORG_ID/FACADE_DEV_USER_ID which nothing reads (leftover from the removed DEV_AUTH_BYPASS era). Readiness checkers in routes/health.py are also never registered, so /readyz equals /healthz.
suggestion: Remove /v1/session and the stale env vars; wire real upstream-reachability checkers into /readyz or drop the parameter.

title: Per-tool call cap has 3+ divergent sources and a dead scaling path
detail: The prompt suffix is baked at import with cap=5 (deep_agent_builder._DEFAULT_TOOL_CALL_BUDGET); RuntimeSettings.execution.tool_call_budget defaults to 6 and is copied onto ModelConfig then depth-scaled (3/6/12) by DepthBudgetTable — but no production code reads ModelConfig.tool_call_budget; actual enforcement is ToolBudgetRecord.max_calls_per_run=6 seed rows via ToolBudgetMiddleware. The model is told 'at most 5' while 6 is enforced, and the Fast/Deep depth scaling of the tool budget is a complete no-op. Docstrings in deep_agent_builder.py:52-54 and depth.py claiming consistency are false.
suggestion: Make the resolved ModelConfig the single owner: derive both the per-run prompt suffix (format_web_subagent_suffix(cap)) and the enforced ToolBudgetRecord cap from it, delete the import-time constant, and add a test asserting prompt number == enforced number.

title: Async subagent lifecycle (~550 LOC + contracts) has no production consumer
detail: AsyncSubagentLifecycle, SubagentRunner, InMemoryAsyncTaskStore (runner.py, 473 LOC), SubagentHandoffBuilder (handoff.py, 79 LOC), and the AsyncTask*/SubagentTask/SubagentResult contract family (~300 LOC of contracts.py) are imported only by the package __init__ and tests. Real delegation is in-process via the deepagents task-tool monkey-patch (atlas_task_tool.py).
suggestion: Delete runner.py, handoff.py, the AsyncTask contracts and their tests (or move to a clearly-labeled experimental area); keep SubagentDefinition/DynamicSubagentCatalog/FilesystemPermissionSpec which are live.

title: Domain modules import the API layer (runtime_api.schemas / agent_runtime.api.ports)
detail: context/memory/subagent_trace.py imports RuntimeEventEnvelope/MessageRecord/event enums from runtime_api.schemas and ports from agent_runtime.api, inverting the documented 'agent_runtime is pure domain' split, and duplicates runtime_worker's StreamTextHelper verbatim to dodge the resulting cycle. pricing/refresh_loop.py and upsert_planner.py import agent_runtime.api.ports too. The boundary test only bans retired legacy paths, not layering direction, so the inversion is unguarded.
suggestion: Move event-envelope/message contracts into a domain-owned schemas module that runtime_api re-exports; extend test_import_boundaries.py to forbid agent_runtime -> runtime_api|runtime_worker imports.

title: Supervisor prompt contradicts itself on checkpoint discipline
detail: DEFAULT_INSTRUCTIONS still instructs 'pause and emit a short progress checkpoint as a plain-text message before calling another tool' — the exact wording deep_agent_builder.py:36-44 documents as causing premature loop termination (a tool-call-free AIMessage is treated as the final answer) — while the harness-profile suffix appended to the same assembled prompt mandates the opposite ('Do NOT emit a checkpoint without an accompanying tool call').
suggestion: Update DEFAULT_INSTRUCTIONS to the corrected same-message wording, or delete its checkpoint paragraph and let the suffix own the contract.

title: Training opt-out is a silent no-op for Anthropic and Gemini
detail: The Anthropic opt-out emits a speculative 'anthropic-disable-training' header the comment admits is a placeholder; Gemini's mapping is an empty dict; and _NO_PROVIDER_FLAG — created so middleware could log the gap — is defined but never used, so the promised warning never fires. A workspace/user setting training_data_opt_out=true gets no effective provider-side signal on 2 of 3 providers, which is a compliance-claims risk for regulated buyers.
suggestion: Verify current Anthropic/Google contractual mechanisms, wire the absent-flag warning or an audit-row annotation, and surface the limitation in Settings copy.

title: runtime_config drops trace_metadata for root runs (probable indentation bug)
detail: In execution/runtime.py:59-61 the metadata.update(MetadataRedactor.redact(context.trace_metadata)) call is nested inside 'if context.parent_trace_id is not None:', so root runs (the common case) never merge their trace metadata into LangGraph config/observability. No test pins either behavior.
suggestion: Dedent the update so trace_metadata merges unconditionally (or document why child-runs-only is intended) and add a root-run assertion.

title: ConfiguredRuntimeGraph, FeatureFlag, and unused contract helpers are unreachable
detail: ConfiguredRuntimeGraph (graph.py:48-117) has no production consumer — langgraph.json exports create_graph -> UnconfiguredRuntimeGraph and only one unit test touches it. FeatureFlag + AgentRuntimeContext.feature_flags round-trip through run records but gate nothing anywhere in src. _normalize_scope and _coerce_iterable in contracts.py are never called.
suggestion: Delete ConfiguredRuntimeGraph and the unused helpers; either wire feature_flags into capability gating or drop the field from the context contract.

title: Import-time global mutation and env reads in the domain layer
detail: install_atlas_task_tool() monkey-patches deepagents.middleware.subagents._build_task_tool at factory.py import time (mirrored upstream code breaks silently on a deepagents refactor, acknowledged in the file), and runtime_checkpointer keeps a process-global singleton whose backing (InMemorySaver vs desktop AsyncSqliteSaver) is decided by direct os.environ reads plus a hard-coded index/checkpoints.sqlite3 path that must mirror FileStoreLayout by hand.
suggestion: Add an upstream-shape assertion test pinning the mirrored deepagents surface; inject checkpointer configuration via RuntimeSettings instead of module-level env reads.

title: BudgetReservationManager docstring promises methods that do not exist
detail: The class docstring claims it 'coordinates reserve / consume / release / reap on top of the port' with 'every method async', but the class only carries a TTL constant and an expires_at classmethod; the _Port marker class in the same module is an empty stub. Real reservation logic lives in the enforcer/charger and adapters.
suggestion: Shrink the module to the TTL + expires_at helper and rewrite the docstring, or actually move the coordination methods in.

title: ReasoningDepth wire values hand-duplicated three times (Python + two TS unions)
detail: The Fast/Balanced/Deep string values exist as the Python StrEnum (depth.py, with a comment saying api-types 'must stay in lockstep') and as TWO separate TypeScript literal unions: ReasoningDepth in packages/api-types/src/index.ts:1314 and AgentReasoningDepth in packages/api-types/src/agents.ts:99. Nothing ties them together.
suggestion: Collapse the two TS aliases into one exported type and add a contract test (or codegen) asserting the Python enum values match.

title: SIEM export pump is unwired and its SQL doesn't match the audit schema
detail: SiemExportPump is instantiated by no composition root, script, or test (repo-wide grep: only its own module + __init__ re-export). If started, _fetch_local queries 'WHERE id > %(after_id)s' against mcp_audit_events/identity_audit_events whose PK is audit_id (no id column), and EventNormalizer reads row['id']/row.get('event_type') where the real column is 'action' — first tick would raise. Pump also never reads siem_exporter_controls, so the /v1/siem pause/resume/replay admin routes are no-ops despite their docstring claiming the pump consults them. Three deployment profiles declare siem_export_required=True; the control cannot function. Pump has zero tests (test_siem_export covers exporters/normalizer with synthetic rows whose keys don't match the real schema).
suggestion: Wire the pump into a composition root or dedicated worker entrypoint, fix cursor SQL to audit_id/action columns, honor siem_exporter_controls in tick(), add an integration test running one tick against the real migrated schema.

title: Audit list/export return empty under Postgres-backed deployments; deploy audit is in-memory only
detail: AuditReader gates each stream on hasattr(store,'list_audit_events')/'list_skill_audit_events' — only the in-memory stores implement these; PostgresMcpStore/PostgresSkillStore have append_audit but no list method, so under DATABASE_URL (production, desktop) /internal/v1/audit/list silently drops the mcp/skill/deploy streams without even flagging degraded_streams. /internal/v1/audit/export reads the in-memory-only 'audit_events' attribute ('Postgres-backed export is a follow-up'). DeployAuditService has no persistent adapter anywhere, including the desktop composition root (explicit accepted gap). Per the repo's own compliance rules, audit export must be considered not implemented for production-style deployments.
suggestion: Implement list_audit_events/list_skill_audit_events on the Postgres stores and a PostgresDeployAuditStore; surface missing readers in degraded_streams instead of returning silently empty.

title: LibraryIndexerLoop is never started by any composition root — embeddings queue never drains
detail: LibraryService enqueues library_index_jobs rows (and memory embeddings ride the same queue by design), but LibraryIndexerLoop is constructed only in tests/test_library_indexer.py. No process (app.py, desktop_app.py, scripts, compose) starts the loop, so library/memory embeddings never materialize in any real deployment and hybrid search silently degrades to BM25-only.
suggestion: Start the loop from a composition root or worker entrypoint gated on LIBRARY_INDEXER_ENABLED, with a boot test asserting it runs.

title: create_app is a 1,620-line, ~60-parameter composition mega-function
detail: All route registration, ~30 store/service wirings, mid-function imports, an inline cross-domain bridge class (_RoutineProjectAllowlistBridge), monkey-patching a sibling service's private attribute (routines_service._project_allowlist_lookup), and repeated noqa'd private reaches (projects_service._membership_port, session_service._auth_secret) live in one 2,048-line file with load-bearing registration-order comments. Every feature edits this file.
suggestion: Decompose into per-domain wiring modules composed by a thin create_app; promote private-attribute contact points to explicit constructor ports.

title: _default_token_vault swallows the factory's fail-closed errors
detail: TokenVaultFactory.create deliberately raises when a KMS-required profile resolves a local vault or KMS env is missing, but app.py wraps it in 'except Exception: return None' — a misconfigured production deploy boots without OIDC/MFA/provider-keys/webhooks routes instead of refusing to start, converting a fail-closed control into a silent feature outage.
suggestion: Catch only the dev no-secret case; re-raise under production/managed profiles.

title: SIEM exporter env var maintained under two names
detail: Exporter factory and docs/security/siem-export.md use SIEM_EXPORTER_BACKEND; the /v1/siem admin routes and docs/roadmap/00-overview.md use SIEM_EXPORT_BACKEND / SIEM_EXPORT_BACKEND_NAMES. An operator following the security doc configures an exporter the admin list endpoint cannot see, and vice versa.
suggestion: Single name constant in copilot_service_contracts; alias the other for one release.

title: Two DB-URL env contracts inside one process (DATABASE_URL vs BACKEND_DATABASE_URL)
detail: Runtime stores and desktop composer resolve DATABASE_URL, while the SIEM admin routes 503 unless BACKEND_DATABASE_URL is set and open raw per-request psycopg connections bypassing the shared pool; operator scripts also use BACKEND_DATABASE_URL. A deploy configured only with DATABASE_URL has working stores but dead /v1/siem/* routes.
suggestion: One env name with explicit fallback; route SIEM admin queries through PostgresConnectionPool.

title: store.py re-implements the chained audit-append pattern five times
detail: _sign_mcp_audit/_sign_skill_audit/_sign_deploy_audit are structurally identical; the Postgres append_audit/append_skill_audit bodies (advisory lock → head read → sign → insert) are copy-pastes; _json_list/_json_object/_datetime/_connect/_connect_or_inherit are duplicated verbatim between PostgresMcpStore and PostgresSkillStore; the three list_*_audit_events filter blocks are triplicated; AuditChainSigner.from_env is rebuilt per append. ~350-450 LOC reducible.
suggestion: Extract a generic ChainedAuditTable(table, payload_fields) helper shared by all three streams and both adapters.

title: Platform modules hand-mirrored across services (deployment profile, observability, pool metrics, env parsers)
detail: deployment_profile.py is mirrored in backend-facade (230 LOC) and ai-backend; the observability quartet (log_config/log_event/request_context/otel) is mirrored in backend-facade and partially ai-backend; pool_metrics self-describes as a mirror of ai-backend's; env_int/env_float parsing is re-implemented three times within this cluster alone. These mirrors carry security-sensitive redaction and fail-closed logic that must be fixed in 2-3 places; packages/audit-chain is the repo's own precedent that cross-cutting primitives may be shared packages.
suggestion: Promote deployment-profile and observability primitives into a shared package alongside audit-chain (contract-stable, truly cross-cutting).

title: Dead shims and unused hooks: migrations.py, OTel instrumentors, HttpOAuthTokenExchanger; plus non-constant-time service-token compare
detail: backend_app/migrations.py (legacy SQL-constants shim) has zero importers; TelemetryBootstrap.instrument_httpx_clients/instrument_psycopg are never called; HttpOAuthTokenExchanger is a pure rename shim whose exchange_code just calls super(). Related minor risk: BackendServiceAuthenticator compares ENTERPRISE_SERVICE_TOKEN with '!=' instead of hmac.compare_digest, and LocalTokenVault.decrypt catches (InvalidToken, Exception) routing all failures through the legacy XOR path.
suggestion: Delete migrations.py and the rename shim; call or remove the instrumentors; use hmac.compare_digest; narrow the decrypt except clause to InvalidToken gated on the Fernet prefix.

title: render_adapter_generator (~1,443 LOC) has no production consumer
detail: capability.py/models.py/templates.py are imported only by their own tests; the only non-test reference elsewhere is a comment in runtime_api/schemas/common.py:181. Its contract dependency copilot_service_contracts.adapter_allowlist is likewise consumed only here plus tests, so the dead chain crosses into shared-packages.
suggestion: Delete the package, its tests, and the allowlist contract; if surface-renderers still needs constrained adapter codegen, capture it as a spec, not shipped code.

title: Dynamic tool-loading subsystem (registry/loader/load_tool/runtime_gate, ~520 LOC) is unwired
detail: DynamicToolRegistry, ToolLoader, LoadToolSpecTool, and ToolUsePolicyGate have zero production consumers; the worker's real registry is WebSearchToolRegistry (runtime_worker/dependencies.py:41). handlers/run.py:784 probes display_for via getattr, but no production registry implements it, so even that never reaches DynamicToolRegistry.
suggestion: Wire it (it mirrors the live MCP dynamic-load design) or delete registry/loader/load_tool/runtime_gate, keeping cards.py/permissions.py/privacy.py which are live contracts.

title: Third code-execution subsystem (code_sandbox + code_tool_adapter, ~690 LOC) superseded and unreferenced
detail: The in-process AST-gated exec sandbox and its adapter have only test consumers, while two live execution subsystems (interpreter/ Monty and sandbox/ remote) now exist in the same cluster. Three parallel run-code designs in one package.
suggestion: Delete both plus tests; keep interpreter/ and sandbox/ as the two sanctioned execution paths.

title: Pure-domain agent_runtime imports runtime_api.schemas at runtime in five capability modules
detail: tool_budget_guard.py:25, citations.py:22, backends/draft_backend.py:32, render_adapter_generator/capability.py:23 import RunRecord/RuntimeApiEventType from the HTTP layer at runtime; citation_resolver.py:108 late-imports with a comment admitting it dodges a circular import. tool_budget_guard/tool_budget_middleware also TYPE_CHECK against runtime_worker.tool_call_ledger. The dependency direction is inverted relative to the documented module split.
suggestion: Move RuntimeApiEventType/RunRecord (or a minimal event-envelope protocol) into agent_runtime contracts and have runtime_api.schemas re-export; the late-import workarounds then disappear.

title: Four near-identical BaseTool wrappers + three near-identical registry decorators
detail: ToolBudgetGuardedTool, ToolErrorPolicyTool, CitationCapturingTool, and RetryingTool each re-implement the propagate-name/description/args_schema + override _run/_arun decorator; ToolBudgetGuardedRegistry, ToolErrorPolicyRegistry, CitationCapturingRegistry each re-implement list_available_tools + idempotent _wrap; display_metadata adds a fifth structurally different wrap. Composition order lives only in runtime_worker/dependencies.py:129-131, and each wrapper handles args_schema=None / injected tool_call_id / idempotency slightly differently.
suggestion: Introduce one DelegatingTool base + a wrap_registry(inner, *wrappers) composer; each concern becomes a small hook, removing ~150-250 LOC and centralizing schema propagation.

title: MCP dispatcher re-implements the citation ordinal-hint block inline
detail: mcp/middleware/call_tool.py:156-205 duplicates citation_capturing_tool.py:139-181 (allocator lookup, allocate_for_tool_call, _CitationHint.append_to, identical warn/info log taxonomy) and imports the private _CitationHint; cite_mcp.py is additionally a pure pass-through alias for CitationProjector.
suggestion: Extract a CitationAnnotator.annotate(result, connector, tool_name, tool_call_id) used by both the capturing tool and the dispatcher; delete the alias class.

title: mcp/files.py (459 LOC) file-based MCP config layout is test-only
detail: The McpServerConfigFile/McpToolMetadataFile/secret-shape scanner subsystem is exported via mcp/__init__ but has no production consumer; McpFileLayout has no consumer at all.
suggestion: Delete, or relocate under the desktop file-store adapter if file-native MCP config is on the desktop roadmap.

title: Three divergent interrupt-resume decision parsers
detail: AuthMcpTool._resume_result (auth_mcp.py:116-138), InterruptApprovalGate._approved (interpreter/policy_invoker.py:259-287), and AskAQuestionTool._resume_result (ask_a_question.py:154-196) accept different decision vocabularies; only the interpreter gate understands the batch {decisions:[{type:...}]} shape, so identical UI resume payloads can approve one interrupt kind and silently reject another.
suggestion: One shared ResumeDecision.parse(resume) helper for all interrupt-based tools.

title: Byte-identical service-auth header builders and token estimators
detail: BackendMcpServiceAuth.headers (mcp/backend_provider.py:427-440) and BackendSkillServiceAuth.headers (skills/virtual.py:215-228) are byte-identical ENTERPRISE_SERVICE_TOKEN builders; the 4-chars/token 100k-cap estimator exists both as _Estimator (tool_budget_guard.py:162-180) and _estimate_input_tokens (runtime_worker/capability_tool_wiring.py:72-81).
suggestion: One BackendServiceAuth next to BackendHttpPool; export the estimator from tool_budget_guard and reuse it in the worker bridge.

title: call_mcp_tool swallows unexpected exceptions without logging
detail: CallMcpTool.ainvoke's final handler `except (McpClientError, Exception):` (call_tool.py:123) converts every unexpected exception into a generic retryable failure with no log, unlike McpLoader's equivalent path which logs warning with exc_info (loader.py:212-217); a systematic client bug would be invisible in production.
suggestion: Log unexpected exceptions before returning the typed failure and drop the redundant exception union.

title: RBAC is default-permissive and no deployment ever enables enforcement
detail: RbacMode defaults to 'audit' — RequireScopes/RequireRoles log denies then PASS THROUGH (identity/rbac.py:42-50,155-180). Repo-wide, no deploy artifact (deploy/self-host, docker-compose, Makefile, desktop supervisor) sets RBAC_MODE=enforce, and deployment_profile has no RBAC toggle, so every scope/role annotation in backend (and the mirrored ai-backend layer) is advisory in every shipped configuration; only mfa:pending denial and facade authentication actually block.
suggestion: Drive RBAC mode from the deployment profile (enforce for production-class profiles) or flip the default to enforce; expose active mode in /v1/health.

title: PostgresSiweStore wired nowhere — wallet identities are in-memory in production composition roots
detail: desktop_app.py (the ONLY Postgres composition root, used by the packaged desktop and deploy/self-host/docker-compose.prod.yml:108) never wires siwe_store, so create_app falls back to InMemorySiweStore while migration 0035_siwe.sql creates tables nothing uses. Wallet→user links vanish on every backend restart: with self-signup on, a returning wallet is re-provisioned into a fresh personal org each restart (org/user pollution, user loses their workspace); with self-signup off, wallet login breaks outright.
suggestion: Wire siwe_store=PostgresSiweStore(pool) in desktop_app.build_create_app_kwargs and add a test asserting every store with a Postgres adapter is wired there.

title: SIWE EIP-4361 template hand-mirrored in three languages; docs claim two
detail: The byte-frozen template lives in backend siwe.py:290-302, frontend siweMessage.ts:30-41, AND desktop local-login.ts:78-91 (root CLAUDE.md documents only the first two). CHAIN_NAMES is additionally mirrored in the frontend LoginScreen. Copies are byte-identical today (verified) but no cross-language fixture test pins all three together.
suggestion: One shared golden-message fixture asserted by all three test suites; fix the CLAUDE.md count.

title: HMAC bearer codec implemented three times in-repo
detail: identity/sessions.py _BearerCodec (sign+verify), dev_idp/_sign.py (sign), backend_facade/auth.py (sign+verify) all implement the same base64url(payload).base64url(hmac) scheme. Claim names are shared via service-contracts, but dev_idp/routes.py:142-150 hand-types literal keys (including 'iat', absent from the shared contract) instead of CLAIM_* constants.
suggestion: Promote the ~40-LOC stdlib codec into packages/service-contracts, or at minimum use CLAIM_* constants in dev_idp.

title: Two parallel dev-mint paths with different revocability
detail: POST /v1/dev/identity/mint (dev_idp) issues 365-day session-less bearers with no sid claim — unrevocable, invisible to session listing/sweeping — while POST /internal/v1/auth/sessions/dev-mint issues profile-gated, session-backed 24h bearers via SessionService.dev_mint. The 'dev exercises production verification unchanged' claim holds for HMAC only, not session binding.
suggestion: Make the dev IdP mint delegate to SessionService.dev_mint so dev bearers carry sid and ride the same revocation path.

title: Default backend image entrypoint wires amnesiac in-memory identity stores
detail: Dockerfile CMD is backend_app.app:app whose defaults are InMemoryIdentityStore/InMemorySessionStore/etc. (app.py:493+); only the self-host compose overrides CMD to backend_app.desktop_app:app. An operator running the published image as-is gets sessions and users that evaporate on restart with no boot-time refusal — unlike TokenVaultFactory, which fails closed in production.
suggestion: Production-profile guard refusing in-memory identity/session stores under BACKEND_ENVIRONMENT=production, mirroring the token-vault fail-closed pattern.

title: settings/schema.sql duplicates migration 0033 and is read by nothing
detail: settings/schema.sql repeats the tenant_settings DDL from migrations/0033_tenant_settings.sql (same table, CHECK list, RLS policy, GRANT); no loader references it, and its CREATE POLICY targets a table that never gets ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY in the same file. Two hand-maintained DDL copies will drift.
suggestion: Delete settings/schema.sql or reduce it to a doc pointer at the migration.

title: RBAC dependency layer duplicated across backend and ai-backend
detail: RbacMode/RequireScopes/RequireRoles/public_route + audit-on-deny + the MFA_PENDING rule are fully mirrored between backend_app/identity/rbac.py and runtime_api/rbac.py (self-described mirror). Scope strings are shared via service-contracts but the deny semantics and mode resolution must be kept in sync by hand.
suggestion: Extract the pure mode-resolution + scope-evaluation core into packages/service-contracts, leaving FastAPI glue per service.

title: LocalTokenVault.decrypt swallows all errors into the legacy XOR path
detail: decrypt catches (InvalidToken, Exception) — Exception subsumes everything — so any failure, including a kms_v1: envelope mistakenly routed to the local vault after a backend switch, falls into the legacy XOR decode and surfaces as an unrelated base64/signature error instead of a clear wrong-vault diagnostic.
suggestion: Catch InvalidToken only; raise CiphertextFormatError early when the ciphertext carries the kms_v1: prefix.

title: Service-token comparison is not constant-time
detail: BackendServiceAuthenticator._verify_service_token compares with 'supplied != expected' (backend_app/auth.py:88-90, the authenticator this whole cluster rides on), while every other secret comparison in the cluster uses hmac/secrets.compare_digest.
suggestion: Use hmac.compare_digest for the service-token check.

title: Circular layering: agent_runtime domain imports runtime_api HTTP layer; runtime_api imports runtime_worker
detail: 17 of 28 agent_runtime/api modules import runtime_api.schemas and/or runtime_api.http.errors; agent_runtime/capabilities, persistence, and context modules also import runtime_api.schemas; runtime_api.app imports RuntimeWorker and system_skills imports runtime_worker.dependencies; fork/mcp services import runtime_worker.audit. The de-facto domain records (ConversationRecord, RunRecord, RuntimeEventEnvelope) live in runtime_api.schemas but are consumed by every layer, so the documented 'agent_runtime = pure domain / runtime_api = HTTP shell' split does not hold at the import graph.
suggestion: Move record/envelope contracts and RuntimeApiError into agent_runtime (contracts module), keep runtime_api.schemas as wire-only request/response types, relocate WorkerAuditEmitter and BUILTIN_SKILLS_ROOT out of runtime_worker so imports point one direction.

title: ai-backend RBAC defaults to log-only audit mode; deny audit-row hook is dead code
detail: RBAC_MODE defaults to 'audit' (log-and-pass-through) and is set in no deploy config (self-host prod compose, docker-compose.dev, desktop runtime, env examples), so RequireScopes(admin:budgets), RequireAnyScope(audit:read, admin:users), and admin:audit-export on the SIEM cursor never actually 403. The best-effort audit branch reads app.state.runtime_audit_appender which is assigned nowhere. Facade-side checks may mitigate, but the service's own gate is advisory in every shipped deployment.
suggestion: Default RBAC_MODE to enforce outside development (mirroring the service-token fail-closed posture) and either wire runtime_audit_appender or delete the dead branch.

title: Per-user inbox SSE bus is process-local while the production image runs 4 gunicorn workers
detail: InboxEventBus is the in-memory singleton only (docstring admits Postgres bus is a 'planned follow-up'), but the Dockerfile CMD runs gunicorn with WEB_CONCURRENCY=4 and production runs a separate worker process. Approval assigned/resolved events published in one process never reach /v1/agent/me/inbox/stream subscribers connected to another process — silent cross-process event loss for a shipped surface.
suggestion: Back the inbox channel with Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY (runtime_inbox_v1) like the run bus, or pin WEB_CONCURRENCY=1 and document the constraint until it lands.

title: inbox_producer.py + inbox_fallback.py (~670 LOC) have no production consumers
detail: HttpInboxProducer/InboxProducerFactory and InboxFallbackScheduler (with presence/tenant-settings ports) are imported only by each other and tests/test_run_inbox_routing.py (which uses NullInboxProducer). No factory call or wiring exists anywhere in src/ — the backend inbox-items lane they target is not connected.
suggestion: Wire the fallback scheduler into the approval flow it was written for, or delete both modules until the backend lane ships.

title: Usage aggregation implemented 4+ times across routes and service layer
detail: UsageApiRoutes._rows_by_day/_rows_by_model/_connector_rows_from_calls/_connector_rows_from_rollup in routes.py are the same accumulate-into-defaultdict loop with different keys; UsageQueryService.rollup_user/connector/subagent/purpose/org_rows plus _RollupBucket classes re-implement it for the rollup job; AgentUsageRoutes._aggregate does it again with a comment admitting the duplication.
suggestion: A single generic bucket-by-key aggregation utility in UsageQueryService would delete ~250 LOC and make the token-column list one edit site.

title: SSE poll-backstop contract diverges from code; bus fallback_poll_seconds attribute is dead
detail: EventBusBackend.fallback_poll_seconds (2s in-memory, 10s postgres, with the postgres docstring promising a 10s backstop) is never read by RuntimeSseAdapter, which hardcodes FALLBACK_POLL_SECONDS=2.0 — every connected client re-runs the replay query every 2s even on the LISTEN/NOTIFY bus; the attribute is only asserted in tests. Relatedly the run stream emits no keepalive frames in follow mode while the inbox adapter sends ': keepalive' every 25s claiming it 'matches the runtime SSE'.
suggestion: Have the adapter use event_bus.fallback_poll_seconds and add the same keepalive comment frame to the run stream.

title: routes.py (1,777 LOC) mixes five routers with business logic that belongs in the service layer
detail: UsageApiRoutes performs persistence-port orchestration, cold-start fallback synthesis, and multi-query joins inline in handlers; BudgetApiRoutes does record construction/merge against the port directly — bypassing the coordinator/app.state-service pattern every other surface in the same package uses.
suggestion: Split usage and budget routes into their own modules (matching retention_routes.py) and push fallback/rollup orchestration into UsageQueryService.

title: /readyz always reports ready — no readiness checkers are registered
detail: register_health_routes(app) is called with no readiness_checkers and CheckResult/Checker have no other producer; the docstring itself says checkers can be passed 'once those wirings exist'. Orchestrator probes will route traffic to instances whose store/queue/event-bus is down.
suggestion: Register store (SELECT 1 via lifecycle port) and event-bus-listener checkers at boot.

title: Python version signals disagree: image 3.14, pyproject 3.13, pyright 3.11
detail: The production Dockerfile uses python:3.14-slim-bookworm while pyproject requires-python is >=3.13 (matching the monorepo 'Python 3.13 everywhere' rule) and pyright targets pythonVersion 3.11 — three different version contracts for one service.
suggestion: Align all three on 3.13 (or deliberately move to 3.14 everywhere and update the repo rule).

title: Tracked junk in service root: Oops.rej, binary .coverage, duplicate env templates
detail: Oops.rej (a leftover patch-reject hunk) and .coverage (53KB binary coverage DB) are committed to git; env_example and .env.example are duplicate tracked copies of the same template.
suggestion: Delete Oops.rej and .coverage, keep one env template, add the patterns to .gitignore.

title: ~3.2k LOC of jobs/ background workers wired to no entrypoint
detail: RoutineSchedulerLoop (+ routine_pre_fire_gate), TodoExtractor, ProposalExtractor, TodoRecurrenceMaterializerLoop and ApprovalExpirySweeper are instantiated only by tests; __main__.py starts only usage-rollup/retention/db-metrics/pricing loops. Meanwhile runtime_api serves the todo-extraction accept/reject API (app.py:222) over a table no producer fills, and approvals never expire because the sweeper never runs.
suggestion: Wire the loops into __main__.amain behind their existing *_ENABLED env gates (they were designed for that) or delete/park them; log started/skipped loops at boot.

title: Approval-resume path skips usage, budget, audit, reconciliation and timeout
detail: RuntimeApprovalHandler._complete_run_with_result never calls usage_recorder.record_run/record_call, budget charge_run, emit_run_completed/emit_run_failed, or _reconcile_inflight_tool_calls, and _stream_resume has no asyncio.timeout (run path wraps at run.py:1302). Runs that pause for approval and then finish are unmetered, unbilled, unaudited and can hang the worker forever.
suggestion: Extract a shared RunCompletionFinalizer (usage + audit + budget + reconciliation + termination) used by both handlers; wrap resume streaming in the same timeout.

title: Serial worker loop; cancel cannot preempt an in-flight run
detail: run_forever→run_once awaits each claim inline (loop.py:118-170), so max_parallel_runs/_claim_batch/semaphore concurrency is exercised only by tests (run_until_idle). A queued RUN_CANCEL_REQUESTED for the currently-streaming run is not claimed until it finishes, and neither StreamingExecutor.run nor the run handler checks CANCELLING mid-stream — worst on desktop where a single in-process worker exists.
suggestion: Dispatch claims as tasks under the existing semaphore in run_forever; poll run status (or a cancellation flag) inside the streaming loop to abort promptly.

title: agent_runtime and runtime_api import runtime_worker (layering inversion)
detail: agent_runtime/api/self_fork.py:27 and conversation_fork.py:25 runtime-import runtime_worker.audit.WorkerAuditEmitter; runtime_api/system_skills.py:30 imports BUILTIN_SKILLS_ROOT from runtime_worker.dependencies; tool_budget_guard.py type-imports ToolCallLedger. Package graph is circular (worker → agent_runtime/runtime_api → worker), contradicting the documented 'agent_runtime = pure domain' split.
suggestion: Move WorkerAuditEmitter, BUILTIN_SKILLS_ROOT and the ToolCallLedger protocol into agent_runtime; worker imports them.

title: Run vs approval handler duplicated flows synced by hand
detail: _workspace_snapshot_emitter is byte-identical in both handlers; _dependencies_for_run/_dependencies_for_resume re-implement the same augmentation incl. duplicated DraftBackend construction; DRAFT_UPDATED payload built independently twice; final-response persistence duplicated; approval.py reaches into RuntimeRunHandler private classmethods (_build_tool_display_lookup, _is_action_interrupt, _extract_final_text). This drift class already produced Bug R1 (documented in file_store_wiring.py) and finding F2.
suggestion: Shared per-run wiring/completion module (same pattern as FileStoreWorkerWiring) consumed by both handlers.

title: Consent-card risk labels from tool-name substring heuristics; irreversible-token list unused
detail: _connector_action_is_read_only marks any tool lacking create/post/send/update/delete/write substrings as read-only → risk_level=low, reversible=NOT_APPLICABLE (e.g. remove_member, merge_pr, drop_table, execute_query render as low-risk reads). _APPROVAL_IRREVERSIBLE_TOKENS (delete/remove/drop) at stream_events.py:951 is defined but never referenced. Approval is still required but the card understates risk to the human.
suggestion: Prefer MCP tool annotations (readOnlyHint/destructiveHint) when present; fail toward write/medium for unknown verbs; wire or delete the irreversible-token list.

title: Per-run in-memory state never purged in long-lived worker
detail: StreamMessageProcessor._tool_call_states/_tool_call_ids and StreamUpdateProcessor._subagent_lifecycle_keys/_subagent_name_by_call_id/_subagent_call_id_by_subgraph_id/_unlinked_subagent_call_ids are never swept per-run (discard_ledger/discard_metrics clear only ledgers/buffers/metrics); RuntimeApprovalHandler._resumed_task_ids grows forever. Unbounded growth keyed by run_id/call_id in a process meant to run for weeks.
suggestion: Add a discard_run(run_id) on StreamOrchestrator that sweeps all per-run keys, called from the three terminal sites in the run handler and from the approval handler.

title: Nine copies of env_float/env_int/env_bool plus seven hand-rolled periodic loops
detail: Near-identical env parsing helpers exist in 9 classes across the service (5 in this cluster) plus a tenth _positive_float/_positive_int variant in dependencies.py; UsageRollupLoop, RetentionSweeperLoop, RoutineSchedulerLoop, TodoRecurrenceMaterializerLoop, ApprovalExpirySweeper and the pricing/db-metrics loops each hand-roll the same start/stop/_run asyncio.Event lifecycle.
suggestion: One EnvReader and one PeriodicLoop base in agent_runtime; each loop reduces to a tick() implementation.

title: Production capability validation guard is unreachable
detail: DefaultRuntimeDependenciesFactory._validate_capability_mode always early-returns because WebSearchToolRegistry().list_available_tools() unconditionally returns a 1-tuple, so the CONFIGURATION_ERROR branch guarding 'no capability sources in production' can never fire.
suggestion: Validate configured external sources (MCP/skills URLs) only, or delete the guard.

title: Two parallel rrule/recurrence evaluators (bespoke where a library would do)
detail: CronSpecEvaluator (routine_scheduler.py:113-330) and RecurrenceRuleEvaluator (todo_recurrence_materializer.py:76-244) implement the same RFC-5545 FREQ/BYDAY/INTERVAL subset with identical _Weekday tables — acknowledged as 'parallel' in the scheduler's own docstring; both hand-roll parsing that croniter/dateutil.rrule provide. Also action_interrupt_events frozenset duplicated in run.py:115 and streaming_executor.py:212.
suggestion: Single shared schedule-evaluator (or croniter/dateutil) if the jobs are wired — the F1 wire-or-delete decision comes first.

title: Web Connectors OAuth surface is a permanent stub in every deployment
detail: POST /v1/connectors/{slug}/start-oauth resolves app.state.connector_oauth_start, which no composition root sets, so it always returns the hard-coded https://auth.example/... stub URL; POST /v1/connectors/oauth-callback always 503s; ConnectorsService.write_through_from_mcp (the only writer of connector rows) has no caller, so the installed-connectors list is permanently empty. The frontend calls these routes via connectorsApi.ts. The desktop variant is real; the web variant never got wired.
suggestion: Wire connector_oauth_start/callback to McpRegistryService.start_auth/complete_auth in create_app and call write_through_from_mcp from the callback leg (the desktop coordinator shows the shape); until then gate the web Connect button on capability detection.

title: All destination stores are in-memory in every shipped composition — product data lost on restart
detail: No Postgres adapters exist for todos/inbox/routines/connectors/webhooks/projects/templates/library/memory/agents/tools/palette (Postgres*Store classes exist only for identity/settings/MCP/adapter-registry). The SaaS image default CMD boots backend_app.app:app with all-in-memory defaults; desktop and self-host boot desktop_app which lists every destination store as an 'accepted desktop-v1 gap'. Ten package schema.sql files have no migration; migrations/0032_todos.sql creates a table nothing reads.
suggestion: Ship Postgres adapters for launch-critical destinations (todos/projects/memory first), generate migrations from the module schema.sql files, and quarantine or delete the rest of the aspirational DDL.

title: Six copy-pasted SSE bus/adapter stacks (~2,400 LOC), three streams with no producers
detail: inbox/memory/tools/connectors/home/team each re-implement the identical in-memory bus (deque + asyncio.Condition + per-channel cursors), SSE adapter loop (heartbeat math, disconnect check), LastEventIdResolver, and constants — inbox/sse.py explicitly says it is 'a copy of the home SSE discipline'. Only inbox, memory, and team ever publish; /v1/tools/stream, /v1/connectors/stream, and /v1/home/stream emit heartbeats forever.
suggestion: Extract one generic tenant-channel bus + SSE adapter + Last-Event-ID resolver parameterized by event name/envelope; then wire producers for tools/connectors/home or delete those streams.

title: agents/installs.py (1,058 LOC) routes are never registered anywhere
detail: register_agent_install_routes (installs.py:652) has no caller in app.py, desktop_app.py, or the facade; agents_routes.py on the facade proxies only CRUD. Only tests/test_agent_installs_routes.py mounts it manually, so install/uninstall/disable/duplicate is unreachable product surface despite being fully implemented and tested.
suggestion: Register it in create_app and add facade proxies (it looks finished), or move it out of the shipping tree until its phase lands.

title: Palette index has no writers — /v1/palette/search is always empty
detail: PaletteRefreshDispatcher is constructed and stashed on app.state.palette_dispatcher, but no destination service ever calls upsert_entry/delete_entry (repo-wide, only tests construct PaletteEntry). The FE wires the Cmd-K palette to /v1/palette/search via PaletteSearchPort, which will always return zero hits in every deployment.
suggestion: Add dispatcher calls at the create/update/delete boundaries of destination services (exactly what refresh.py's docstring prescribes), or replace the index with federated live queries.

title: notifications/dispatcher_gate.py (260 LOC) consumed only by its own test
detail: Repo-wide grep finds no production import of dispatcher_gate/DispatchGate — only tests/test_notification_dispatcher_gate.py. The notifications package is otherwise store-only, with its routes in routes/notifications.py.
suggestion: Delete it or wire it into the future notification dispatch path; today it is speculative code carrying test-maintenance cost.

title: Per-destination CRUD service scaffolding copy-pasted across 9+ modules
detail: Each destination re-defines XNotFound/XForbidden/XInvalidRequest exception trios, a _can_read 404-not-403 predicate, _validate_create/patch_payload, _safe_dump, module-local _now(), and _ADMIN_ROLES = frozenset({'admin','owner'}) — the frozenset alone appears in 14 files. Route layers repeat the same exception-to-status translation.
suggestion: Introduce a shared destination_common module (exception bases, admin-role constant, dump/validation helpers, exception-to-HTTP mapper) to single-source the 404-not-403 rule and cut several hundred LOC.

title: Todo recurrence grammar implemented twice across services with no shared contract
detail: RecurrenceRuleEvaluator (rrule FREQ/BYDAY/INTERVAL subset + every_N_days + every_weekday) exists in backend todos/service.py:86 and ai-backend runtime_worker/jobs/todo_recurrence_materializer.py:94. The comment claims they 'cannot drift unobserved' but nothing mechanically enforces lockstep — no shared constants, no cross-service golden fixtures.
suggestion: Move the grammar constants plus a golden test-vector file into packages/service-contracts so both implementations validate against one fixture set.

title: Settings routes put business logic at the route layer, contradicting the destination layering rule
detail: Destination modules mandate 'routes are presentation-only; logic lives in service.py', but routes/me_preferences.py does deep-merge + re-validation + deployment defaults inline, routes/notifications.py does matrix hydration + audit-row writes inline, and privacy/tool_use_policies follow suit — the backing packages (notifications/policies/privacy) are store-only with no service layer.
suggestion: When these settings gain a second caller or grow, lift merge/hydration/audit into small services matching the destination pattern; not urgent today.

title: Hand-maintained duplicate facts: todos DDL, webhook HMAC constants, connector slug metadata
detail: (a) migrations/0032_todos.sql is a declared 'verbatim copy' of todos/schema.sql kept in sync by hand; (b) webhooks/signer.py claims every signer/verifier reads algo+header names from it, yet routines/webhook.py hard-codes SIGNATURE_PREFIX='hmac-sha256=' and its own header names; (c) connector slug/display metadata is maintained in connectors/catalog.yaml, mcp_catalog.DEFAULT_CATALOG, and desktop_profiles.yaml with boot-time reconciliation.
suggestion: Generate the migration from the module schema (or drop one copy), import the HMAC prefix/algo constants in routines/webhook.py from signer.py, and make the profile-catalog reconciler the single loader for slug metadata.

title: Postgres adapter missing 3 PersistencePort approval methods — live endpoint 500s
detail: list_assigned_approvals, list_pending_expired_approvals, and list_pending_approvals_for_membership_audit are declared on PersistencePort (agent_runtime/api/ports.py:342,357,365) and implemented by the in-memory and file adapters, but are entirely absent from PostgresRuntimeApiStore. list_assigned_approvals is live: runtime_api/http/routes.py:354 → approval_coordinator.py:140 → persistence — on RUNTIME_STORE_BACKEND=postgres the assigned-approvals inbox raises AttributeError (HTTP 500). The structural Protocol compiles and boots cleanly, and the cross-backend conformance suite explicitly excludes Postgres (test_store_conformance.py:3-4), so nothing catches it.
suggestion: Implement the three methods in the Postgres adapter and add a static conformance test asserting every adapter structurally satisfies every port Protocol (isinstance against runtime-checkable Protocols needs no database).

title: Citation store wiring is split-brain on file and in-memory backends
detail: RuntimePorts has no citation_store field, so RuntimeWorker improvises: citation_store = persistence if isinstance(persistence, CitationStorePort) else InMemoryCitationStore() (runtime_worker/loop.py:74-80). Only Postgres passes the check. On the desktop file backend the worker writes citations to a private throwaway InMemoryCitationStore while the factory wires the Sources feed to read FileCitationStore (factory.py:93-106); on the in-memory backend the feed reads a second fresh instance (factory.py:191). Result: Workspace Sources is always empty off-postgres, FileCitationStore durability is never exercised, and citation rebuild-on-resume after worker restart is impossible.
suggestion: Add citation_store to RuntimePorts, wire the correct implementation per backend in the factory, pass it explicitly into RuntimeWorker/handlers, and delete the isinstance fallback.

title: File adapter re-implements the in-memory adapter's logic nearly verbatim (~11k LOC across 3 stores)
detail: The file store was written as a fork of the in-memory store: same materialized-view dicts, same method bodies, plus JSONL write-through. charge_budget is byte-identical between in_memory/runtime_api_store.py:1835-1870 and file/runtime_api_store.py:2473-2510 except a lock and a ledger call; the pattern repeats across budgets, reservations, usage rollups/queries, pricing, approval batches, tool budgets, and queue semantics. base.py shares only ~400 LOC. Every port change is a triple hand-sync (quadruple counting the Postgres SQL).
suggestion: Make the file store compose the in-memory store (shared pure state-transition core + journaling write-through decorator per table family); plausibly 1,500-2,000 LOC reduction and eliminates a whole class of drift.

title: PersistencePort is a ~90-method god interface implemented three times
detail: agent_runtime/api/ports.py:85-927 spans conversations, messages, runs, approvals, approval batches, audit, history deletion, workspace defaults, usage recording, pricing, five daily-rollup families, budgets, tool budgets, and retention in one structural Protocol. Incomplete implementations compile, import, and boot (see F1). The satellite ports in persistence/ports.py already demonstrate the right granularity.
suggestion: Split into cohesive role ports (ConversationStore, ApprovalStore, UsageStore, BudgetStore, RetentionStore...), compose them in RuntimePorts, and enforce per-port conformance in tests including Postgres.

title: Retention/deletion semantics silently diverge per backend with no capability signal
detail: Postgres implements chunked tombstone/hard-delete sweeps for all 8 RetentionKinds plus retention_until backfill/recompute (postgres/runtime_api_store.py:3649-4303). The file store collapses every kind into whole-conversation physical purge keyed on MESSAGES and stubs backfill/recompute as return 0 (file/runtime_api_store.py:2656-2722). The in-memory sweep is an explicit no-op (in_memory:1027-1053). delete_user_history tombstones on postgres/in-memory but erases bytes on file. Divergence is documented per adapter but invisible to callers and compliance review.
suggestion: Publish a per-backend retention/deletion capability matrix and/or expose a capabilities descriptor on the lifecycle port so the sweeper and compliance tooling can assert what a deployment actually enforces.

title: ~2k LOC of file-store product surface built and tested but mounted nowhere
detail: export_conversation/import_conversation (file/runtime_api_store.py:1744-1814 + export_import.py, 569 LOC), search_conversations FTS (:858-893 + search.py + FTS in _catalog_index.py), store_health/conversation_health/needs_repair_ids (:370-413), and verify_audit_log (:1615) have zero consumers in src, no HTTP route, and no desktop IPC — only unit tests exercise them. (repair.py and runtime_adapters/migrate.py are real operator CLIs with docs and are not dead.)
suggestion: Mount search/export/health through runtime-API routes consumed by the desktop shell, or explicitly mark them staged with a tracking issue.

title: Canonical record models live in runtime_api — domain and adapters import the HTTP layer
detail: ConversationRecord/MessageRecord/RunRecord and siblings are defined in runtime_api.schemas, so the 'pure domain' persistence package and all adapters import upward: agent_runtime/persistence/message_copy.py:27, runtime_adapters/base.py:8-14, file/runtime_api_store.py:120-140, plus 27 files across agent_runtime importing runtime_api. Contradicts the documented module split (agent_runtime = pure domain; runtime_api = presentation).
suggestion: Move core record models into agent_runtime.persistence.records (or a contracts module) and have runtime_api.schemas re-export for wire compatibility.

title: Unwired adapters: todo-extraction stores, share-snapshot store, legacy schema shim
detail: InMemoryTodoExtractionStore (93 LOC) and PostgresTodoExtractionStore (185 LOC) implement TodoExtractionStorePort but no production code constructs either — the todo_extractor job's extraction_store parameter is itself unwired; only route tests use the in-memory one. InMemoryShareSnapshotStore (45 LOC in src) is consumed exclusively by tests (the app registers share_service as the snapshot port). PERSISTENCE_TABLE_RECORDS (records/__init__.py:94-107) has zero consumers, and schema/postgres.py's legacy SQL shim (91 LOC + import-time reads of three SQL files) is test-only.
suggestion: Wire the todo-extraction store into RuntimePorts when the feature ships or relocate the adapters beside their tests; move the share-snapshot double to test fixtures; delete PERSISTENCE_TABLE_RECORDS and fold the schema shim into its test.

title: Default tool budget seed (max_calls_per_run=6) maintained by hand in three places
detail: The seed_default ToolBudgetRecord (tool_name='*', max_calls_per_run=6, hard enforcement) is duplicated in in_memory/runtime_api_store.py:167-174, file/runtime_api_store.py:271-279, and the SQL seed in migrations/0010_runtime_tool_budgets.sql:37-41 (whose comment ties it to a fourth copy, the RUNTIME_TOOL_CALL_BUDGET default). A drifted edit silently gives dev/desktop different budget behavior than production.
suggestion: Define the seed once in persistence/constants.py (or settings) and reference it from both adapters; assert the SQL seed matches in a schema test.

title: Uneven extras on the shared adapter surface (seed helpers, undeclared file-store extensions)
detail: seed_approval_request exists on the in-memory (in_memory/runtime_api_store.py:2212) and file (:1567) production classes but not Postgres — a test helper living on the shared surface. The file store's desktop-only methods (search, export/import, health, verify_audit_log) widen its de-facto interface beyond the ports with no declared extension protocol — the same structural-typing drift vector that produced F1.
suggestion: Move seeding helpers to test fixtures; declare a FileStoreExtensions protocol for the desktop-only surface so consumers gate on it explicitly.

title: CLI ships Electron 42.1.0 while the desktop app builds and tests on 43.1.1
detail: tools/cli/package.json pins electron 42.1.0 as the runtime end users launch, but apps/desktop/package.json pins 43.1.1 for dev, CI, and electron-builder releases. The payload's out/ bundle is built against the 43 toolchain yet executed on 42's Node/Chromium via the npm path — a released surface running on an Electron major nothing in CI exercises.
suggestion: Make the desktop app's electron version the single source (read it at assemble/prepack time or add an equality lint in ci-cli.yml) and bump the CLI to 43.1.1.

title: Boot contract implemented twice (run-local.mjs vs desktop supervisor) and already drifted
detail: run-local.mjs hand-mirrors the env/spawn sequence of apps/desktop/main/services/service-env.ts, and the README claims the supervisor spawns exactly what run-local spawns — but the supervisor now sets SIWE_ORIGIN and FACADE_WEB_DIST_DIR (the PRD-2 wallet fix) while run-local sets neither, so the smoke no longer proves the wallet-page serving that was this stack's confirmed production-down bug.
suggestion: Add the web-dir/SIWE env and a GET /wallet.html assertion to run-local, or extract the desktop-profile env table into a shared spec both sides consume.

title: Zero behavioral tests; tools/desktop-runtime has no CI coverage at all
detail: No *.test.* files exist in any of the three tool dirs; ci-cli.yml runs only node --check + help smoke + manifest check, and no workflow path-filters tools/desktop-runtime/** (ci-desktop covers only apps/desktop + packages), so stage.mjs/run-local.mjs breakage first surfaces on a release tag or a user machine. Untested code includes rm -rf-adjacent logic in uninstall.mjs and repair.mjs whose bugs destroy user data.
suggestion: Unit-test isSafeTarget/pid-parsing/process-matching with zero-dep node:test; add node --check + a path filter for tools/desktop-runtime; consider a scheduled run-local.mjs job on macos-14.

title: COPILOT_DESKTOP_USER_DATA_SUBDIR has no consumer — harness dev posture shares prod userData
detail: driver.mjs sets COPILOT_DESKTOP_USER_DATA_SUBDIR='cli-test-dev' claiming 'a separate userData dir so the dev session never collides with prod', but a repo-wide grep finds no consumer anywhere: dev-posture smoke runs share the real ~/Library/Application Support/0xCopilot secrets/pgdata and can pollute a genuine install on the same machine.
suggestion: Implement the env var in desktop main (app.setPath('userData',...) before ready) or delete the dead line and isolate via COPILOT_HOME.

title: Manual, unreproducible npm publish folds a gitignored OAuth credential file into the public tarball
detail: There is no publish workflow for @0x-copilot/cli; prepack's assemble-payload.mjs copies the gitignored apps/desktop/google-oauth.json (which may contain client_secret per apps/desktop/.gitignore comments) into payload/desktop/, so published-package content depends on uncommitted maintainer-local state with no CI attestation or secret guard.
suggestion: Publish from a workflow using env-synthesized client id only (Desktop-app PKCE clients need no secret) and fail prepack if google-oauth.json contains client_secret.

title: SERVICES + SHARED_PACKAGES staging tables hand-mirrored between stage.mjs and assemble-payload.mjs
detail: assemble-payload.mjs re-declares the per-service copy-dir lists and shared-package set that stage.mjs owns (its own comment says 'Mirror tools/desktop-runtime/stage.mjs SERVICES'). A service adding a staged dir must be edited in both or published payloads silently lack source the stager expects.
suggestion: Export the tables from a small shared staging-spec module imported by both.

title: Third hand-copy of the SIWE EIP-4361 message template in the smoke harness
detail: siwe-session.mjs re-implements the 'Sign in to Copilot' statement and full EIP-4361 layout that CLAUDE.md documents as a byte-identical PAIR (frontend siweMessage.ts + backend siwe.py) — the harness is an undocumented third sync point that fails silently at verify time when the template changes.
suggestion: Have the harness import the frontend's siweMessage builder (acceptable for test tooling) or at minimum add it to the documented sync set.

title: Constant/path facts duplicated across the cluster and into desktop-app/CI
detail: ~/.cache/enterprise-desktop-runtime is hand-maintained in cli paths.mjs, stage.mjs, and release-desktop.yml; default state dir ~/.0xcopilot in paths.mjs and driver.mjs; APP_NAME/BUNDLE_ID hand-synced with branding.ts and electron-builder.yml; isSafeTarget duplicated verbatim in repair.mjs and uninstall.mjs. All comment-documented, none machine-checked.
suggestion: Hoist isSafeTarget and branding constants within tools/cli; pass the cache dir to the stager via a flag instead of a parallel constant.

title: Dead CLI surface: needsStage() export, unused existsSync import, no-op repair --yes
detail: needsStage() is exported from tools/cli/lib/stage.mjs but imported nowhere (logic inlined in stageRuntime); bin/copilot.mjs imports existsSync unused; repair accepts a --yes flag it never uses while help text advertises it as prompt-skipping.
suggestion: Delete the dead export and import; scope --yes to uninstall in the help text.

title: Stale 'broken wallet.html' claim in siwe-session.mjs header
detail: The harness header still says it 'BYPASSES the broken facade-served wallet.html', but FIX-VERIFICATION.md records PRD-2 (wallet 404 + SIWE origin) as fixed and live-retested (GET /wallet.html -> 200), so the comment misleads readers into believing the primary login path is still dead.
suggestion: Reword to 'drives SIWE headlessly without the wallet page UI'.

title: AC8 agentic-browser subsystem (~3.4k LOC) built and tested but wired nowhere
detail: main/index.ts never imports main/browser; esbuild.config.mjs has no build task for the claimed out/browser-worker/index.js worker bundle; the supervisor never sets DESKTOP_BROWSER_BROKER_URL/TOKEN that the ai-backend's DesktopBrowserMcpProvider requires. browser/index.ts's docstring falsely claims main builds the subsystem when isDesktopBrowserEnabled is true — a compliance reader would wrongly conclude the desktop has an egress-policied browser capability.
suggestion: Land the wiring slice (worker build task + main gate + env delivery to ai-backend child) or move the subtree to a marked incubating area and correct the docstring.

title: AC5 capability broker token/URL never delivered to ai-backend — host-folder grants dead end-to-end
detail: startCapabilitySubsystem mints the per-boot broker token, and the renderer grant UI/picker/broker all run, but buildServiceEnv sets no DESKTOP_BROKER_URL/DESKTOP_BROKER_TOKEN and the passthrough allowlist strips them, while workspace_backend.py waits on exactly those vars. Users can mint folder grants that nothing can honor; index.ts admits 'slice 2 wiring' is pending.
suggestion: Have the supervisor consume the broker handle and inject url+token into the ai-backend child env; until then gate the renderer grant UI off with the same flag.

title: Destination projections hand-duplicated between web routes and desktop destinationBinders
detail: bucketConversations/chatStatus/toArchiveRow and mapRunStatus/auditLabel/buildMetaIndex/projectActivityRows exist twice — apps/frontend features (chatsApi/activityApi, tested) and apps/desktop/renderer/destinationBinders.tsx (untested). These are pure functions over @0x-copilot/api-types shapes with zero substrate dependency; the in-file comment acknowledges the duplication as intentional but the shared home (chat-surface, next to the consuming components) already exists.
suggestion: Lift the projections into packages/chat-surface beside the destination components; both hosts import them and the desktop copies gain the web copies' tests for free.

title: Curated cloud-model catalog hardcoded again in desktop composer, already divergent from web
detail: desktopModelCatalog.ts hardcodes a 4-model curated cloud list mirroring the web ChatScreen's separately hardcoded list (which includes gpt-5.4-nano the desktop lacks) — another instance of the known 7+ divergent hardcoded model lists. The file's own OQ4 note anticipates a served catalog.
suggestion: Serve one model catalog from the facade (or at minimum one shared constant in api-types) and delete both hardcoded lists.

title: Tier-2 harvest/download/loader/opt-out modules consumer-less; install pipeline idles on stub event source
detail: main/adapters/{harvest,download,loader,opt-out}.ts (~660 LOC + ~1030 test LOC) are imported only by each other and their tests. The wired lifecycle runs on StubLifecycleEventSource ('Phase 7's concern'), so the Q1-Q5 install path receives no events at runtime; only the boundary-error->mark-broken path is live.
suggestion: Delete or park the four consumer-less modules until the community-registry backend exists; keep the wired pipeline.

title: boot-secrets writes ENTERPRISE_AUTH_SECRET/pg password as plaintext fallback unconditionally
detail: When safeStorage is unavailable, boot-secrets persist() silently falls back to chmod-600 plaintext JSON with no allowPlaintextFallback gate and no user-visible warning — whereas sibling stores (SecretStorage ATLASv1, GrantStore ATLASCAPv1) fail closed unless the explicit dev flag is set. Deliberate for headless Linux but divergent from the app's own convention and invisible to the user.
suggestion: Emit a visible degraded-security signal (log + boot status) and align the three stores on one policy knob.

title: Triplicated encrypted-blob envelope, env-flag parser, and loopback-broker hardening
detail: Three near-identical safeStorage marker-prefixed blob implementations (ATLASv1/ATLASBOOTv1/ATLASCAPv1), three copies of the TRUTHY env-flag set (capabilities/feature-gate, browser/feature-gate, service-env), and the AC8 browser-broker re-implements the AC5 broker's transport hardening (~300 LOC acknowledged mirror).
suggestion: Extract one encryptedBlobStore helper and one isFlagEnabled helper; share a loopback-broker base only if AC8 ships.

title: auth.get-posture IPC channel has no renderer consumer; deep-link app-login path never wired
detail: The auth.get-posture handler is registered and documented as letting SignInGate hide the dev-mint option, but no renderer production code invokes it (SignInGate always shows all three options; safety holds because main routes local sign-in by posture). registerDeepLinks' onOAuthCallback branch is never provided by main — a non-connector enterprise://oauth/callback logs a warning and drops, contradicting the loopback-vs-deep-link race described in deep-links.ts and oidc docstrings.
suggestion: Consume posture in SignInGate (label/hide the local option) or drop the channel; update the stale docstrings in rpc-protocol.ts and deep-links.ts.

title: Browser MCP tool JSON Schemas hand-mirror the Zod protocol schemas
detail: main/browser/tool-schemas.ts hand-authors JSON Schemas that must stay in sync with protocol.ts's Zod definitions by discipline alone (acknowledged in-file, no codegen). Latent drift risk that only matters if/when AC8 ships.
suggestion: Generate the JSON Schemas at build time from the Zod source (zod-to-json-schema as devDependency, output checked in).

title: Minor consolidation: 622-line composition root, duplicated allowPlaintext expr, doubled readiness probes
detail: main/index.ts computes the allowPlaintext expression twice (capability subsystem and auth wiring); RunBinder and RunComposer independently probe /v1/settings/provider-keys + /v1/local-models on mount, doubling requests and risking divergent readiness verdicts in the same cockpit. destinationBinders.tsx (691 LOC) is also the largest untested renderer file.
suggestion: One shared readiness hook for the Run cockpit; hoist allowPlaintext to a single helper; add projection tests when lifting them into chat-surface (F3).

title: docs/unused-code/backend/ is a second audit of ai-backend; services/backend never audited
detail: docs/unused-code/backend/README.md titles itself 'Unused code and smells — services/ai-backend' and explicitly scopes OUT services/backend and backend-facade, duplicating docs/unused-code/ai-backend/ with a different 12-cluster bucketing and separate vulture artifacts (same date 2026-05-06, same revision a1d79d7). The folder name misleads any consumer, and no dead-code audit of services/backend (~120k LOC) exists anywhere.
suggestion: Merge both passes under docs/unused-code/ai-backend/, rename or delete the backend/ folder, and record explicitly that services/backend is unaudited (candidate for a fresh pass).

title: use-cases README indexes 14 docs; 13 of the links are broken
detail: docs/use-cases/README.md's scenario index links 01-cold-start-first-message.md, 08-user-cancels-mid-stream.md etc.; the folder actually contains 13 differently-named docs (01-new-conversation-simple.md, 05-cancel-run-mid-stream.md, ...). Only 06-mcp-installed-not-authenticated.md matches. It also links services/ai-backend/docs/architecture/data-flow.md, which is not tracked in the repo.
suggestion: Regenerate the index from the directory listing; fix or drop the data-flow.md pointer.

title: decomp corpus half-written; indexes promise ~20 missing docs plus two nonexistent companions
detail: docs/decomp/README.md claims a 92-file inventory and cites companion deliverables docs/refactor/ and replacement-analysis.md — neither exists. The eight _index.md files link ~20 per-file docs that were never written (capabilities/mcp-loader.md, runtime-worker/handlers-approval.md, persistence/persistence-bundle.md, ...). Consumers following the index hit missing files more often than not.
suggestion: Mark missing docs 'not yet written' in each index or write them; delete the refactor/replacement-analysis references until those artifacts exist.

title: Workspace-layout facts drifted across four hand-maintained copies
detail: docs/architecture/workspace-topology.md and .cursor/rules/{service-boundaries,workspace-architecture}.mdc still describe apps/mac + apps/windows as the planned clients and omit apps/desktop, apps/website, packages/{chat-surface,chat-transport,surface-renderers,audit-chain,service-contracts}, and tools/* — all listed as implemented in root CLAUDE.md. docs/architecture/service-boundaries.md still marks apps/desktop 'Planned' and the substrate decision 'gated on Phase S spike' though desktop-app.md records it decided 2026-05-17 and the app ships.
suggestion: Regenerate topology + mirrors from CLAUDE.md, or add a generation script so mirrors cannot drift.

title: multi-tenant-deployment.md lists RLS/KMS-vault/SIEM as planned; sibling runbooks document them implemented
detail: docs/architecture/multi-tenant-deployment.md's 'Planned / design-only tracks' still says Postgres RLS is 'not enabled in application DDL today' and lists managed token vault and SIEM export as future, while docs/security/rls-incident-response.md (C5), key-rotation.md (C6), field-encryption.md (C7) and siem-export.md (C9) are operational runbooks for those shipped controls. A stale 'planned' label is a compliance-review hazard in either direction under this repo's evidence-first rules.
suggestion: Update the implemented/planned matrix to reference the C5-C9 runbooks and re-verify each remaining 'planned' row.

title: The two ai-backend dead-code passes contradict each other on share_service._collect_sources
detail: docs/unused-code/ai-backend/06 marks the sources_visible parameter HIGH-confidence redundant (remove or fold redaction inside); docs/unused-code/backend/cluster-agent-runtime-domain-services.md rules the identical claim 'False positive ... no remediation needed', citing call-site reads at ~lines 459/468 of share_service.py. A refactor agent trusting the first doc ships a change the second argues is wrong.
suggestion: Resolve against current code once during the F1 merge and delete the losing claim.

title: Salvaged high-confidence prior dead-code claims for re-verification at HEAD
detail: Surviving concrete claims from the 2026-05-06 audits: ai-backend runtime_api/schemas/inbox.py::InboxEventEnvelopeSchema (zero imports; inbox SSE hand-builds the same wire shape in sse/inbox_adapter.py — also an SSOT violation), runtime_adapters/factory.py from_settings(migrate=) never-read parameter, ToolBudgetMiddleware.check_admit not invoked from the production path; facade TelemetryBootstrap.get_tracer/get_meter and StructuredLogger.debug/.exception unreferenced, register_health_routes(readiness_checkers) unwired so /readyz is always ready, _raise_for_upstream x3 and settings_for x4 copies, product-api-surface.md route drift; frontend §4a export-hygiene sweep (~20 symbols) and §5 type-export inflation (~85 types) still open (§1/§2/§4b already deleted at a78bfc0).
suggestion: Have the verification fleet re-verify this short list (not the 635-line vulture dumps) against HEAD before any deletion PRs.

title: Broken pointers in living docs: dev-testing 'Run Locally' README section; desktop-app.md links a machine-local memory path
detail: docs/dev-testing.md's Pointers cite 'Root README — ## Run Locally section', but README.md was rewritten as the public product README and has no such section. docs/architecture/desktop-app.md line 54 links substrate reasoning to ../../.claude/projects/-Users-parthpahwa-Documents-work-0x-copilot/memory/... — a contributor's machine-local Claude-memory path that can never resolve in the repo.
suggestion: Point dev-testing at CLAUDE.md's Commands section; inline the substrate reasoning summary and drop the memory link.

title: Roadmap and plan corpora carry no shipped-status tracking
detail: docs/roadmap files have no status markers although C1-C12 are evidenced shipped by ADR-0002 and the deployment/security runbooks; docs/plan/desktop/PRD.md still reads 'draft for review 2026-05-17' while agent-capabilities/README.md says 'current code wins over stale phase labels'; ~43 of 59 new-design statuses read 'Draft' including shipped waves. Only agent-capabilities/TODO.md tracks done/in-progress/planned correctly.
suggestion: Add one status table per corpus README (id → shipped/partial/planned + evidence link) in the TODO.md style.

title: ~70k of ~89k doc LOC is unmarked historical planning text
detail: new-design (31.7k) + atlas-new-design (13.1k) + pre-AC plan/desktop (~14.9k) are delivery history for shipped or superseded work, sitting beside living contracts with equal prominence; supersession is recorded only in the newest layer (DEV-GUIDE, PLAN). new-design/0-OVERALL_PLAN and atlas-new-design/PRD reference design bundles at /tmp/... paths that do not exist in-repo.
suggestion: Add ARCHIVED/HISTORY banners + a corpus index distinguishing delivery-history from current contracts; do not delete (they are the decision record).

title: apply-branch-protection.yml can never run — top-level `return` SyntaxError in inline Python
detail: The heredoc script uses `return` at module level in four places; Python fails at compile time on every invocation, including dry-run. Branch protection therefore cannot be applied or diffed via the documented path — the ruleset in git is unenforced aspiration.
suggestion: Wrap the body in main() or move it to deploy/scripts/apply_branch_protection.py with a pytest smoke; add py-compile/actionlint pre-commit coverage for inline workflow scripts.

title: CI runs zero tests for frontend, chat-surface, chat-transport, surface-renderers
detail: ci-frontend only typechecks and builds; the only `npm run test` in any workflow is desktop's. 157 frontend + 186 chat-surface test files (plus chat-transport/surface-renderers suites) never execute in CI, despite chat-surface being the SSOT interaction layer for both web and desktop.
suggestion: Add vitest steps for frontend + the three packages to ci-frontend (or a ci-packages workflow with matching path filters).

title: Staged-rollout production gate is guaranteed-fail for non-canary tenants
detail: deploy.yml synthesizes prior deploys with tier/environment "unknown", but check_staged_rollout.py filters on tier==upstream and environment=="production" — never matching. Early/general promotions always exit 1 and can only proceed with force_deploy=true, making the bypass the default path the policy exists to prevent.
