suggestion: Persist real tier/environment per deploy run and reconstruct them, or make the step warn-only until the durable audit query API exists so --force stays exceptional.

title: CI/release path filters drifted from the real dependency graph
detail: ci-frontend triggers only on apps/frontend + api-types + design-system, but frontend source imports @0x-copilot/chat-surface 169x and chat-transport 3x — a chat-surface change breaking the frontend build merges without ci-frontend running. release-images omits packages/audit-chain although backend + ai-backend Dockerfiles pip-install it, so an audit-chain-only change publishes no new images.
suggestion: Add packages/chat-surface, chat-transport, surface-renderers to ci-frontend and packages/audit-chain to release-images; adopt ci-desktop's documented filter-mirrors-dependencies convention.

title: Supply-chain rigor asymmetric: ai-backend has no hash pinning
detail: backend and facade use requirements.in → pip-compile --generate-hashes, CI reproducibility verification, and --require-hashes installs in CI and Dockerfiles. ai-backend — the largest dependency surface (LangChain/DeepAgents) — has no requirements.in, no hashes, plain pip install in both CI and its Dockerfile.
suggestion: Bring ai-backend onto the same pip-compile + --require-hashes lane.

title: Shipped images run Python 3.14 while everything tests on 3.13 (pyright pinned to 3.11)
detail: All CI jobs, service venvs, and the desktop runtime pin Python 3.13, but all three service Dockerfiles use python:3.14-slim-bookworm — production runs an interpreter minor no suite exercises. pyrightconfig.json additionally says pythonVersion 3.11.
suggestion: Pin images to python:3.13-slim (or move CI to 3.14) and set pyright to 3.13.

title: Migration-manifest logic triplicated; the documented CI gate does not exist
detail: tools/check_migration_manifest.py duplicates parse/digest/render logic also implemented in services/backend db/migrate.py and services/ai-backend schema/migrate.py (both hard-code the tool's header text). Docs (roadmap C2, decision 0002, runbook) claim it runs in CI, but no workflow, hook, or Make target invokes it — drift is caught only at service boot.
suggestion: Add a check_migration_manifest step to ci-backend/ci-ai-backend (dependency-free) and reduce to one implementation per format concern.

title: branch-protection.json would deadlock merges if ever applied
detail: Required status checks name path-filtered workflows (ci-backend / test-and-audit etc.); PRs whose paths don't trigger them would block forever on never-reporting contexts. All three backend workflows share the job name test-and-audit, so the context strings may not match GitHub's actual check names. bypass_actors is empty.
suggestion: Make required workflows always report (in-workflow change detection instead of path filters) or require only always-on ci-repo jobs; verify context strings on a live PR before applying.

title: DEV_AUTH_BYPASS: CLAUDE.md says it no longer exists, but docker-dev and the facade still use it
detail: docker-compose.dev.yml sets DEV_AUTH_BYPASS=true + FACADE_DEV_ORG_ID/USER_ID; Makefile check-prod-env still guards it; backend-facade deployment_profile.py still honors it under the dev profile. docker-dev runs a different auth posture than make dev's real dev-IdP bearers.
suggestion: Migrate docker-dev to the dev-IdP mint flow and delete the bypass, or correct CLAUDE.md.

title: nginx gateway config duplicated and already drifted (dev SSE dies at 60s)
detail: infra/docker/dev-gateway.conf and the inline gateway config in deploy/self-host/docker-compose.prod.yml are declared mirrors, but only prod has proxy_read_timeout 3600s — docker-dev SSE streams idle beyond nginx's default 60s read timeout get cut, the exact bug class the prod copy guards against.
suggestion: Add proxy_read_timeout to dev-gateway.conf and cross-reference both copies with a change-both marker.

title: Bearer exp claim never verified on the HMAC path
detail: FacadeAuthenticator.verify_identity_token checks signature and claim shape only — no expiry logic exists anywhere in backend_facade/auth.py despite every minted bearer carrying exp (sessions.py:373, dev_idp/routes.py:148). Expiry is enforced only by the session-store row during a sid touch (session_store.py:109-110). Consequence: routes using authenticate_request alone (all /v1/auth/mfa/*, /v1/auth/session, /v1/session, /v1/auth/me/login-attempts) accept expired bearers indefinitely, and sid-less bearers (dev-IdP 365-day mints, or anything signed with a leaked secret) are accepted on every route with no expiry or revocation check because verify_with_touch silently falls back (auth.py:256-259).
suggestion: Add an exp check (one clock comparison against CLAIM_EXPIRES_AT) inside verify_identity_token, or adopt PyJWT which validates exp by construction.

title: REQUIRE_SESSION_BINDING kill-switch implemented but enabled nowhere
detail: The facade can reject sid-less bearers (auth.py:401-411) — closing what its own comment calls the externally-minted-token back-door — but no deployment artifact sets REQUIRE_SESSION_BINDING: not deploy/self-host/docker-compose.prod.yml, not docker-compose.dev.yml, not the desktop supervisor service-env.ts. All real login flows now mint sid-carrying bearers, so the flag could be enabled in both production-posture stacks today.
suggestion: Set REQUIRE_SESSION_BINDING=true in the desktop supervisor env and self-host prod compose once the dev IdP mints sid bearers (see dev-mint finding).

title: Two parallel dev-mint implementations with divergent security semantics
detail: POST /v1/dev/identity/mint (dev_idp/routes.py:111-164) signs directly — no session row, no sid, 365-day TTL — while POST /internal/v1/auth/sessions/dev-mint → SessionService.dev_mint (routes/sessions.py:132-160, sessions.py:257-280) mints a real 24h session gated by the deployment profile. Frontend, desktop OidcClient, and Makefile all use the former, so every dev bearer is unrevocable and (given the exp finding) effectively eternal.
suggestion: Fold the dev IdP onto SessionService.create so dev bearers get sid/touch/revocation semantics and dev_idp/_sign.py is deleted.

title: HMAC bearer codec hand-implemented 3x, twice in the same service
detail: The base64url(JSON).base64url(HMAC-SHA256) codec exists in backend_facade/auth.py (_sign/_b64encode/verify_identity_token, lines 382-412 and 501-513), backend identity/sessions.py _BearerCodec (88-141), and backend dev_idp/_sign.py (26-44). The facade/backend copy is the documented boundary trade-off, but two copies inside services/backend is a plain DRY violation with real drift risk (canonical-JSON and padding rules must agree byte-for-byte for tokens to interoperate).
suggestion: Within services/backend, make dev_idp use _BearerCodec (or SessionService); longer term replace all three with PyJWT.

title: SIWE EIP-4361 template triplicated across two languages, no cross-language conformance test
detail: The byte-exact message template + statement constant live in apps/frontend/src/features/auth/siweMessage.ts:30-41, services/backend/src/backend_app/identity/siwe.py:290-302, and apps/desktop/main/auth/local-login.ts:78-91, each with change-all-together comments. The backend parser rejects any drift (siwe.py:315-451), so a single-side edit bricks wallet + local login at runtime with SiweMessageInvalid; only the frontend copy has a pinning fixture.
suggestion: Add a shared golden fixture asserted by all three test suites (or generate the TS constants from the Python template at build time).

title: Hand-rolled JWT-alike, EIP-4361 parser, and TTL-LRU where maintained libraries exist
detail: The bearer is a bespoke near-JWT with RFC-7519-style exp; PyJWT would give exp/nbf validation (fixing the top finding by construction), kid-based rotation, and collapse the three codec copies. The ~140-line strict EIP-4361 parser (siwe.py:315-451) re-implements the maintained siwe PyPI package; the touch cache (facade auth.py:95-172) duplicates cachetools.TLRUCache.
suggestion: Adopt PyJWT for the bearer format at the next token-shape change; evaluate the siwe package for the parser.

title: Dev-mode fallbacks trust caller-supplied identity (query params / bare headers)
detail: With no ENTERPRISE_SERVICE_TOKEN: ai-backend legacy routes take org_id/user_id from query params (runtime_api/http/routes.py:477-493) and the lenient path promotes bare x-enterprise-org-id headers into TrustedRequestIdentity without any token (runtime_api/auth.py:63-78); backend falls back to query identity (backend_app/auth.py:49,66). All fail closed under *_ENVIRONMENT=production, so dev-only — but it contradicts the repo's own untrusted-identity rule and the strict-vs-lenient dual path inside runtime_api invites new routes onto the wrong one.
suggestion: Migrate remaining legacy routes to the strict Identity dependency and delete scoped_identity's query fallback.

title: One deployment fact, three env vars (BACKEND_/FACADE_/RUNTIME_ENVIRONMENT)
detail: The dev/production trust posture is keyed off three independently-read env vars (backend_app/auth.py:99, backend_facade/auth.py:486, runtime_api/auth.py:127). Both prod artifacts set all three today, but nothing validates consistency — omitting one silently flips that service into dev trust posture while the rest of the stack looks production.
suggestion: Single DEPLOYMENT_ENVIRONMENT name in service-contracts, or a boot-time cross-check that logs/fails on mismatch.

title: Frontend SIWE orchestration duplicated between WalletSignIn and LoginScreen
detail: LoginScreen's v2 sign-in card re-implements the nonce→build→personal_sign→verify→adoptSession ramp and the hex personal_sign helper instead of mounting WalletSignIn (LoginScreen.tsx:237-283, 722-754 admits it mirrors WalletSignIn's private helper; compare WalletSignIn.tsx:120-151, 300-304). WalletHandoffPage correctly reuses WalletSignIn.
suggestion: Extract a headless useSiweSignIn hook consumed by both surfaces.

title: auth_routes.py: 974-line hand proxy with three per-route auth conventions
detail: Each route hand-assembles settings/http_client/auth-call/error-mapping with one of three auth levels chosen by convention (authenticate_request vs verify_with_touch vs anonymous service headers); the MFA routes use the weakest level, so MFA verify itself skips the revocation touch. Generic helpers _backend_get/_backend_post exist but are used inconsistently.
suggestion: Route-table-driven forwarder with an explicit auth-level enum per route; move MFA routes onto verify_with_touch.

title: api-types is a ~9.6k-LOC hand-maintained dual-write of server Pydantic contracts; drift tests cover only 4 enum tuples
detail: README.md and SPEC.md require every public contract change to be hand-dual-written in TS and Pydantic; 54 files in services/backend/src alone self-describe as 'mirrors of api-types'. The only automated cross-language check is ai-backend's test_api_type_contracts.py, which regex-parses index.ts and set-compares RUNTIME_API_EVENT_TYPES / RUNTIME_EVENT_SOURCES / RUNTIME_ACTIVITY_KINDS / AGENT_RUN_STATUSES. Approvals, usage, MFA, audit, settings, and every destination payload can drift with zero signal until the UI misrenders.
suggestion: Generate the TS from the FastAPI/Pydantic OpenAPI output into a generated/ path (SPEC.md already plans this), or at minimum extend the tuple drift-test pattern to every runtime tuple and add per-route response-shape fixtures.

title: All four tier-1 surface renderers register into a registry nothing mounted reads
detail: registerAll() is called only by apps/desktop/renderer/bootstrap.tsx:45; the sole registry reader is resolveAdapter in chat-surface's TcSurfaceMount (thread-canvas), which neither app mounts (DestinationOutlet.tsx notes ThreadCanvas as future work; apps/frontend never imports the package). ~2.4k LOC of adapters + 1.5k of tests currently ship dormant.
suggestion: Mount ThreadCanvas (the known Track-C flagship) or gate registerAll() behind that milestone so the dependency isn't shipped dormant.

title: api-types imports service-contracts' JSON via an undeclared relative-path cross-package import
detail: adapterAllowlist.ts imports ../../service-contracts/src/.../adapter_allowlist.json with no dependency declared in package.json. It only works because packages ship source in one checkout; consumer builds must silently include service-contracts (apps/frontend/Dockerfile:16 copies it with no explanatory comment). Publishing or relocating either package breaks the import invisibly.
suggestion: Vendor the JSON into api-types with a checksum canary against the Python copy, or make the JSON its own tiny package; at minimum declare the workspace dependency and comment the Dockerfile line.

title: Accent and theme unions hand-duplicated between api-types and design-system
detail: api-types UserProfileAccent/UserProfileTheme (index.ts:2887-2899, comment 'Mirrors ACCENT_SCHEMES') and design-system AccentScheme/ThemeScheme/ACCENT_SCHEMES (index.tsx:32-59) are maintained independently; adding an accent requires two edits with only a comment as linkage and no failing check on divergence.
suggestion: Derive design-system's AccentScheme from an api-types as-const tuple (type-only import is safe), or add a tuple-equality canary test.

title: Hand-rolled SSE parser only recognizes LF-LF frame boundaries
detail:
sse.ts:68 splits frames on "

"; the SSE spec also permits CRLF line endings where the boundary is 

, which contains no 

 substring — every event would buffer forever and the stream silently delivers nothing. Works against uvicorn (LF) today, but a CRLF-normalizing proxy breaks streaming with no error. Also bespoke-replaceable (eventsource-parser).
suggestion: Split on /\r?\n\r?\n/ and add a CRLF unit test, or adopt eventsource-parser.

title: IpcTransport hand-validates the shape its own package defines as a zod schema
detail: IpcTransport.#asStreamEvent (IpcTransport.ts:204-225) manually re-implements StreamEventPayloadSchema (rpc-protocol.ts:249-255) from the same package; the two validators can drift when the schema gains a field.
suggestion: Use StreamEventPayloadSchema.safeParse in #receiveStreamEvent, or document the hot-path rationale and add a test asserting both accept/reject identically.

title: Root CLAUDE.md claims audit-chain is 'shared Python + TS' but no TS implementation exists
detail: packages/audit-chain contains only the Python signer; no TS audit-chain exists anywhere in the repo. A compliance reviewer could infer client-side chain verification that doesn't exist.
suggestion: Fix the root CLAUDE.md line (or build the TS verifier if client-side verification is actually planned).

title: surface-renderers hardcodes a dark-only hex palette + px type against the v2 token SoT rule
detail: _shared/palette.ts keeps literal hex neutrals (#101113, #181a1c, …) and EmailRenderer inline styles hardcode px sizes and hex, while design-system CLAUDE.md mandates all consumers resolve var(--color-…) tokens; only the lime→accent slot was reconciled. Renderers will not respond to light/slate themes once mounted.
suggestion: Map the remaining palette entries to design-system tokens (pattern already exists in the same file) before the Track-C mount makes these visible.

title: Six unreferenced font assets (~123KB) ship in design-system
detail: space-grotesk (2) and instrument-sans (4) woff2 files have no @font-face references; styles.css:8-9 itself documents them as 'vendored but unreferenced' after the v2 native-stack switch. Only the two JetBrains Mono files are used.
suggestion: Delete the six files; git history preserves them.

title: surface-renderers declares chat-transport as a runtime dependency used only by a lint-negative fixture
detail: package.json lists @0x-copilot/chat-transport under dependencies, but the only import site is src/__lint-negatives__/imports-transport.ts — a file designed to FAIL lint — and the package's own eslint config bans the import.
suggestion: Move it to devDependencies (needed only so the negative fixture typechecks).

title: SSOT interaction layer has zero CI enforcement; boundary lint currently failing
detail: No workflow runs chat-surface's lint, typecheck, or 186-file vitest suite; ci-frontend.yml doesn't even trigger on packages/chat-surface/** changes, and pre-commit runs prettier only. The substrate-ban eslint — the package's declared 'one hard rule' — fails today with 11 bare-document errors in test files, proving nothing runs it. A regression here ships to BOTH hosts unobserved.
suggestion: Add a CI job triggered on packages/chat-surface/** running lint + tsc + vitest; add an eslint override relaxing no-restricted-globals for *.test.* so the rule stays strict for production code and the lint goes green.

title: Run cockpit opens a second SSE subscription, violating its own FR-3.3 single-projection invariant
detail: TcSwimlanes subscribes directly to /v1/agent/runs/{id}/stream (uncursored — full replay per mount) while useRunSession owns the canonical cursor-resumed stream, and it rebuilds beads with different rules than eventProjector (no state-changing filter, own lane/title derivation). Code comments everywhere declare 'a second projection is a bug'; useRunSession.ts documents this as convergence risk R4. Doubles backend stream load per open run and can desync lanes vs timeline.
suggestion: Pass session.events (or projection.swimlanes.beads) into TcSwimlanes as props and delete its subscription — the projector already computes beads with lanes.

title: Most of eventProjector's output has no production consumer
detail: Only timeline.beads and surface slices are read by ThreadCanvas. The chat entries, activity feed, approvals map, pendingApprovals selector, and SwimlanesConsumer slice have zero non-test consumers (TcChat fetches its own messages; approvals come from approvalProjection; activity tab never wired; TcSwimlanes streams itself). Also unreachable: useRunSession's resolveError branch + toError helper (the run-list catch discards errors). ~300 LOC of well-tested but dead reducer code invites divergent fixes.
suggestion: Either wire the slices (TcChat entries, RightRail activity tab) or shrink the projector to beads+surface and delete the rest.

title: Three parallel reducers per run-event family across package and web host
detail: Approvals: eventProjector's extractApproval/nextApprovalState vs approvalProjection.projectApprovals vs apps/frontend chatModel/approval.ts. Subagents: subagentProjection+subagentHelpers (which literally reproduces the host's chatModel/subagentStatus.ts) vs the host's 314-LOC subagentReducer.ts. Each pair is documented as a 'mirror'. The 'binders cannot share code' rationale only applies apps-to-apps — apps CAN import this package, and the citations family proves convergence works (web wraps the package's linkReducer).
suggestion: Repeat the citations move: make the package selectors canonical, turn host reducers into thin wrappers, delete the duplicated status helpers.

title: Latent approval-decision bug: projector matches 'reject' but the wire emits 'rejected'
detail: eventProjector.nextApprovalState branches on decision === 'reject', but the backend emits past-tense 'approved'/'rejected' (runtime_worker/handlers/approval.py:653; approval_coordinator.py documents 'wire decision values stay past-tense'), so a rejected approval would project as state 'accepted'. Bead pending flags are also never cleared on approval_resolved. Harmless today only because these slices are dead (F2) — a booby trap for whoever wires them. The live approvalProjection path checks the correct values.
suggestion: Fix the string match (or delete per the dead-code finding); source the decision union from api-types' ApprovalDecision instead of literals; replace the _approvals-stub with the AssignedApproval contract that now exists.

title: Settings surface and aui-composer CSS each exist twice (package vs web host)
detail: Desktop mounts the package SettingsSurface + settingsNav SSOT while the web still mounts its legacy SettingsScreen with its own sections list — exactly the duplicated-list situation settingsNav.ts says it replaced. The aui-* composer/workspace CSS lives in both packages/chat-surface composer.css (123 refs) and apps/frontend styles.css (513 refs); composer.css itself calls the web copy 'the de-dup follow-up'. Desktop and web styling/settings can drift silently.
suggestion: Finish the web migration to SettingsSurface; make the web import the package CSS files and delete the styles.css copies.

title: Hardcoded, already-divergent model-ID lists in five places
detail: PROVIDER_CATALOG.models (claude-opus-4), ModelPicker defaults (claude-opus-4-7), Composer DEFAULT_MODEL + id-to-label map, AgentEditor MODEL_OPTIONS (anthropic:claude-opus-4-7), RoutineEditor default — three different spellings of the same model family across one package. Matches the known product-wide '7+ divergent hardcoded model lists' issue.
suggestion: One catalog module (or the facade-served ModelCatalog wire type) feeding all pickers via props — the pickers already accept descriptor props.

title: RightRailTabs (414 LOC), ROUTE_TABLE, and the deprecated registerSurface path are unreachable
detail: shell/RightRailTabs.tsx has no consumers, no test, and no export (RightRail.tsx documents deliberately not using it). routing/route-table.ts's ROUTE_TABLE of stub components is exported but consumed by nothing — its planned Phase-3 replacement happened via the destinations model instead. The deprecated registerSurface/resolveSurface/clearRegistry legacy surface path is still barrel-exported with zero non-test consumers vs the live registerAdapter path.
suggestion: Delete RightRailTabs and ROUTE_TABLE; remove the deprecated surface exports in the next minor.

title: Two coexisting composer depth models for the same fast/balanced/deep concept
detail: ModelPicker's Depth + DEPTHS descriptor list and depth.ts's ThinkingDepth + label/description tables model the identical three-value union with independently-maintained copy; only ThinkingDepth feeds the reasoning_depth wire field. The code flags it itself (FR-1.7, 'reconciliation deferred to Phase 3E; do not silently fork') and the web host carries a third pointer file.
suggestion: Collapse to ThinkingDepth (the wire-connected model); ModelPicker takes descriptors as props or imports the shared table.

title: Stale VS-Code-fork docs in the ports/storage contract files; 'one substrate touchpoint' claim outdated
detail: key-value-store.ts, secret-storage.ts, presence-signal.ts, and SecretStorageProvider still describe the desktop as a VS Code extension (Memento, extension-RPC bridge, SecretStorage API) — the desktop is custom Electron. eslint.config.js and CLAUDE.md claim LocalStorageKeyValueStore is 'the one allowed substrate touchpoint' while nine files legitimately use globalThis.*; one doc says 'defaults to window.localStorage' while the impl uses globalThis. These are the most-read contract files in the package.
suggestion: One doc pass replacing the VS Code framing with the Electron reality and enumerating the sanctioned globalThis touchpoints.

title: TcSwimlanes SSE subscription omits eventName — receives zero events from the real backend
detail: TcSwimlanes opens its own per-run stream subscription without eventName; WebTransport (and the desktop IPC bridge that wraps it) defaults to "message" while the backend frames every event as `event: runtime_event`, so the frame filter drops everything and the swimlane timeline never populates against the live stack. useRunSession documents this exact trap and passes the name explicitly. Fix: remove the second subscription and feed swimlanes from the single projector's beads slice (FR-3.3), or minimally pass eventName.
suggestion: Delete TcSwimlanes' own subscription and derive beads from the shared eventProjector output; if the subscription must stay, pass eventName: "runtime_event" and an after_sequence cursor.

title: Worker dispatch is serial; cancels starve and cancellation never interrupts execution
detail: run_forever→run_once awaits the entire run inside one claim (both prod __main__ and the dev in-process worker), so max_parallel_runs/semaphore never parallelize (run_until_idle is test-only). In a one-worker deployment a cancel command for the in-flight run cannot be claimed until the run finishes; RuntimeCancelHandler then flips the already-COMPLETED run to CANCELLED (no terminal-state guard). With parallel workers, RuntimeRunHandler never checks CANCELLING mid-stream, keeps burning tokens, and its completion write uses with_optimistic_retry which refetches+retries, overwriting CANCELLED→COMPLETED. Clients can see contradictory terminal events.
suggestion: Spawn claims as semaphore-bounded tasks (machinery exists in _handle_claim_with_limit); add a legal-transition guard making terminal statuses immutable in update_run_status; poll a cancellation flag between stream chunks.

title: Event contract hand-mirrored across Python and TS; strict client guard fails closed on unknown types
detail: RuntimeApiEventType (43 values), RuntimeActivityKind, AgentRunStatus, visibility/redaction/source enums exist as Python StrEnums and again as TS unions PLUS const arrays (three copies of the event-type list), synced by hand with no codegen or cross-language test. isRuntimeEventEnvelope rejects unknown event_type/activity_kind, so a backend-first event-type addition is silently dropped by the cockpit (parseEnvelope→null) or raises a protocol error in legacy chat — despite the envelope carrying event_protocol_version for evolution.
suggestion: Generate the TS enums/guards from the Python schemas (or a shared JSON schema exported in CI), and make the envelope guard tolerant of unknown event types (accept + render generically) since activity_kind is the stable rendering key.

title: Three client-side pipelines consume and project the same run event stream
detail: (1) Legacy web chat: ChatScreen + agentApi.streamRunEvents + the ~20-module chatModel reducer family with its own reconnect/backoff and background-run registry. (2) Run cockpit: useRunSession + eventProjector + pure selectors. (3) TcSwimlanes' own subscription + toBead. Approval reduction is implemented three times (chatModel/approval.ts, eventProjector, approvalProjection.ts — whose header admits it mirrors chatModel), and run-status-from-event-type mapping exists in backend _status_for, useRunSession, and chatModel/status.ts. Converging legacy chat onto the chat-surface projector is the biggest LOC/consistency payoff in this flow.
suggestion: Retire the ChatScreen/chatModel stream pipeline in favor of useRunSession+eventProjector (already the declared SSOT), and fold TcSwimlanes onto the projector's beads slice.

title: No keepalive on idle run SSE + no clean-EOF signal or auto-reconnect in the cockpit
detail: RuntimeSseAdapter emits zero bytes while a run idles (e.g. waiting_for_approval), unlike InboxSseAdapter's 25s keepalive whose docstring wrongly claims parity with the runtime SSE. Client side, runSseStream fires no callback on clean EOF and useRunSession only reconnects via manual retry() — a proxy dropping the idle stream freezes the cockpit at status "streaming" mid-run. Legacy ChatScreen has reconnect/backoff but shares the silent-clean-EOF hole.
suggestion: Add the keepalive comment frame to RuntimeSseAdapter's follow loop, add onClose to the SSE runner contract, and auto-resubscribe from latestSequenceRef in useRunSession.

title: useRunSession resolves runs via GET /v1/agent/runs — an endpoint that exists nowhere
detail: The cockpit fires GET /v1/agent/runs?conversation_id= on every mount, but neither the facade nor runtime_api registers a run-list route (only POST /v1/agent/runs → GET is 405). The failure is swallowed by design, so ~120 LOC of tolerant parsing (parseRunList/pickActiveRunId/etc.) and the RunMultiSelect multi-run selector can never populate; auto-resolution of an active run never works.
suggestion: Either implement GET /v1/agent/runs (facade + runtime_api + api-types) or delete the resolution path and RunMultiSelect until the endpoint exists.

title: RuntimeRunHandler.handle is a 340-line method in a 1,667-line class
detail: handle() interleaves budget preflight, seven context-var bind/unbind pairs, streaming-vs-invoke branching, interrupt detection, final-message assembly, citation sealing, and four terminal paths each repeating the discard-ledger/discard-metrics/record-usage/audit choreography. The bind/unbind pairs should be an ExitStack; terminal choreography belongs on the existing RunTerminationCoordinator so new terminal paths cannot forget a step.
suggestion: Behavior-preserving decomposition: context-binding ExitStack helper + a single terminal-path finalizer on RunTerminationCoordinator.

title: In-process worker silently refuses non-in-memory stores; docs advertise it unconditionally
detail: start_in_process_worker returns without any log when RUNTIME_STORE_BACKEND isn't in-memory, while root CLAUDE.md and services/ai-backend/CLAUDE.md advertise RUNTIME_START_IN_PROCESS_WORKER=true for local dev with no caveat. Against Postgres (e.g. desktop's embedded PG), runs queue forever with no worker and no hint why.
suggestion: Log a warning when the flag is set but skipped, and document the in-memory-only restriction.

title: SSE bookkeeping edge cases: shared-waiter unsubscribe, leaked conditions, synthetic heartbeat sequence numbers
detail: One viewer's terminal replay pops the per-run wait primitive shared by all concurrent viewers (they silently degrade to 2s/10s polling); the adapter loop lacks try/finally so client disconnects leak per-run condition entries; non-follow heartbeats fabricate sequence_no=latest+1 that a resuming client could use as a cursor and skip a real event (currently unreachable via the facade since the follow param is not forwarded).
suggestion: Reference-count per-run waiters, wrap the stream loop in try/finally, and stop stamping synthetic sequence numbers on heartbeats (use 0 or a dedicated field).

title: Event replay is unbounded with has_more hardcoded False
detail: replay_events fetches every event after the cursor in one query (no LIMIT in the Postgres adapter) and always returns has_more=False; delta coalescing defaults off (window_ms=0), so long chatty runs make first-connect responses and SSE catch-up replays arbitrarily large even though the response schema already carries a pagination flag.
suggestion: Add a page size to list_events_after and honor has_more; consider enabling a modest delta-coalesce window by default.

title: Nine destination families (~32k LOC) have no live mount on either host
detail: home/, inbox/, todos/, agents/, library/, memory/, routines/, team/, and the old MCP tools/ catalog (incl. its OpenApi/Code/Mcp/Skill onboarding wizards, RoutineEditor at 2,082 LOC) are consumed only by the package barrel and their own tests. Web App.tsx redirects the folded slugs and no longer imports any corresponding feature route (HomeRoute/InboxRoute/TodosRoute/AgentsRoute/LibraryRoute/RoutinesRoute/MemoryGateway/ToolsRoute have zero non-test importers); desktop folds agents/inbox onto activity and placeholders everything else. Web's team profile mounts its own features/team UI, not chat-surface team/. ~10.5k LOC of tests for unmounted UI run in CI.
suggestion: Per family: delete (wire types already live in api-types) or move to an explicit archive outside the barrel; strip the barrel exports so the public API matches the real product surface and CI stops testing dead UI.

title: Web maintains a full parallel Settings implementation vs chat-surface SettingsSurface
detail: apps/frontend/src/features/settings (SettingsScreen.tsx 1,405 LOC + 17 section files: Appearance, LocalModels, ModelAndBehavior, Notifications, PrivacyAndData, Profile, ProviderKeys, Shortcuts, sections.ts) imports zero symbols from chat-surface settings, while desktop mounts the full SettingsSurface + ten pages. Contradicts packages/chat-surface/CLAUDE.md ('both hosts import the bodies from this package's barrel') and settingsNav.ts's claim to have replaced the web section lists. BYOK/appearance/model-behavior/privacy UIs are dual-maintained and diverging.
suggestion: Port the web settings screen onto SettingsSurface + the shared section bodies (desktop's SettingsMount is the template), or explicitly retire the SSOT claim in the package docs.

title: Transitional _*-stub wire types persist in 3 places after canonical api-types landed
detail: Seven _*-stub.ts modules in chat-surface destinations and seven mirrors in apps/frontend/src/api still redeclare wire shapes (e.g. TodoPriority) that packages/api-types/src/{todos,projects,agents,inbox,library,routines}.ts now canonically own, each carrying an unexecuted TODO(merge) rewire marker. The live ProjectsRoute still imports _projects-stub. Three parallel declarations of the same contract with no drift protection.
suggestion: Execute the planned rewire: point remaining live imports at @0x-copilot/api-types and delete all 14 stub files (most fall together with the dead-family cleanup).

title: Finished shared components unadopted while web ships hand-rolled scaffolds
detail: WebhookCreateWizard (831 LOC) is unmounted while web WebhooksRoute keeps a WebhookCreateWizardScaffold 'until the chat-surface WebhookCreateWizard lands'; ConnectorDetailView + its 3 tab components (~1,350 LOC) unmounted while ConnectorDetailRoute renders its own detail; TemplateGallery (466) unmounted while TemplateGalleryRoute reimplements; WebhookSecurityPage (321) unmounted vs web WebhookSecurityPanel; plus ChatsSidebar/ChatsDestination (546), ProjectEditor (896), TemplateEditor (515), fork-from-template + archive-blocked dialogs (796), ProjectFilterChip (459).
suggestion: For each pair, either complete the adoption (swap the web scaffold for the shared component) or delete the shared component — keeping both guarantees drift.

title: Run cockpit is desktop-only; web run slug mounts legacy ChatScreen, duplicating event reducers
detail: Web's run slug renders ChatScreen (App.tsx:838-857), not RunDestination, so approval/subagent event-reduction logic exists twice — approvalProjection.ts itself documents it 'mirrors the host-owned approval reducer in apps/frontend/.../chatModel'. The 'one interaction layer, two hosts' invariant holds for list destinations but not the two biggest surfaces (Run + Settings).
suggestion: Commit web to RunDestination (its I/O is already port-shaped) or record the split as a deliberate decision with a convergence plan; docs currently claim convergence the code doesn't have.

title: Hardcoded model catalogs in PROVIDER_CATALOG (plus dead copies)
detail: settings/data/providerKeys.ts embeds per-provider model lists ('claude-opus-4', 'gpt-4o', 'gemini-2.5-pro', ...) — one live instance of the repo's known 7+ divergent hardcoded model lists (the desktop first-run review already decided on a models.dev-backed catalog). Dead copies also in AgentEditor.tsx:224 and RoutineEditor.tsx:207.
suggestion: Source model options from the single model-catalog port decided in the model-picker workstream; keep PROVIDER_CATALOG for provider identity/key-prefix only.

title: Tolerant hand-rolled response parsers instead of api-types contracts in Run session
detail: parseRunList accepts bare array / {runs} / {items} with snake_case or camelCase fields (useRunSession.ts:387-454) and runIdFromCreateResponse accepts run_id/runId/id flat or nested (RunDestination.tsx:660-677). This permissiveness hides real facade/api-types drift and silently degrades wrong shapes to 'no prior runs'. The stream path shows the right pattern (isRuntimeEventEnvelope guard from api-types). toError in useRunSession.ts:456 is unused in-file dead code.
suggestion: Add RunListResponse/CreateRunResponse types + guards to api-types, pin the facade contract, delete the tolerant parsers and the unused toError helper.

title: Optimistic approval decision can show a false receipt on silent POST failure
detail: resolveApproval overlays the optimistic approved/rejected state then POSTs /v1/agent/approvals/{id}/decision with .catch(() => {}) (RunDestination.tsx:460-468). If the POST fails while the stream is also down — the likeliest joint failure — the user permanently sees an approved/rejected receipt for an approval the server still considers pending, on a consent-critical surface.
suggestion: On POST rejection, roll back the optimistic Map entry and surface a toast through the existing NotificationCenterProvider.

title: Package CLAUDE.md module map misclassifies live vs folded destinations
detail: packages/chat-surface/CLAUDE.md lists 'skills' among folded/legacy destinations though it is the live redesigned surface for the tools slug, and its Settings claim is contradicted by the web's parallel implementation. This file is the onboarding doc for the SSOT pattern, so the drift actively misleads.
suggestion: Refresh the module map to explicitly mark live vs archived families and correct the Settings hosts claim.

title: Barrel accretion: 1,270-line index.ts exports the full dead surface
detail: src/index.ts and settings/index.ts export every dead family and unadopted component with phase-block commentary, obscuring the real public contract and inviting accidental adoption of dead components (e.g. ToolsDestination vs SkillsDestination on the same slug). Fixing the dead-code findings would roughly halve the barrel.
suggestion: After pruning, regenerate the barrel around the six live destinations + settings and pin it with an export-surface snapshot test.

title: Web Connectors destination OAuth is a stub end-to-end; its read model is never populated
detail: POST /v1/connectors/{slug}/start-oauth falls back to _default_oauth_start returning https://auth.example/{slug}/authorize?state=stub and POST /v1/connectors/oauth-callback raises 503 unless app.state.connector_oauth_start/-callback are injected — nothing in the repo sets either. ConnectorsService.write_through_from_mcp (the only populate path) is called solely by unit tests, and only InMemoryConnectorsStore exists (connectors/schema.sql has no Postgres adapter). The live Tools destination (web App.tsx:919, desktop destinationBinders.tsx:348-414) therefore always renders an empty Connected tab and web Connect opens a fake URL, while the real connector state works one screen away via /v1/mcp/servers. Even a successful desktop AC9 connect never appears in /v1/connectors.
suggestion: Wire start-oauth/oauth-callback to McpRegistryService.start_auth/complete_auth and call write_through_from_mcp from complete_auth (plus a Postgres ConnectorsStore), or delete the read model and project /v1/connectors directly off the MCP store.

title: ~10 distinct representations of an MCP server/connector across 3 hand-maintained catalogs
detail: backend McpServerRecord/McpServerResponse/InternalMcpServerCard (contracts.py:257/520/713), CatalogEntry (mcp_catalog.py:31), ConnectorRecord+ConnectorCatalogEntry (connectors/), DesktopConnectorProfile/ResolvedConnectorProfile (profile_catalog.py); ai-backend McpServerCard (cards.py:119), McpServerConfigFile (files.py:304), CatalogSuggestionCard; api-types McpServer (index.ts:32), Connector (connectors.ts), connectors-desktop variants; desktop Zod schemas (schemas.ts); frontend ConnectorRow projection. Catalogs: DEFAULT_CATALOG (13), catalog.yaml (9, overlapping slugs), desktop_profiles.yaml overlay. Two live UIs render the same concept from different models (Settings grid on McpServer vs Tools destination on Connector) with two disjoint, un-mapped status taxonomies.
suggestion: Collapse the two UI worlds onto one wire model (Finding 1 decides which); merge catalog.yaml into DEFAULT_CATALOG-derived data; document the remaining forced boundary mirrors.

title: MCP enum vocabulary hand-copied in three places with no conformance check
detail: McpTransport/McpAuthMode/McpAuthState/McpServerHealth are independently declared in backend contracts.py:148-176, ai-backend cards.py:45-80, and api-types index.ts:18-31. A new state requires three manual edits and drift only surfaces as runtime Pydantic validation failures on the internal-cards path. The Connector status taxonomy (connectors.ts) is a fourth, un-mapped lifecycle vocabulary for the same object.
suggestion: Add a cross-service conformance test (or codegen from a single spec file) that asserts the three enum sets stay identical, and an explicit McpAuthState->ConnectorStatus mapping function.

title: Internal RPC proxy flattens SSE to the first data event, no JSON-RPC id matching, blocking urllib
detail: _decode_remote_mcp_response returns the first non-[DONE] data: line of an event-stream response (service.py:792-803); streamable-HTTP MCP servers that emit notifications (progress/log) before the response would have the notification returned as the tool result and the real response dropped. The transport is synchronous urllib with a 30s timeout inside the request path (service.py:766-790), so every agent tool call is a blocking ai-backend->backend->remote double hop through the threadpool.
suggestion: Iterate SSE events until the message whose jsonrpc id matches the request id; move the proxy to httpx.AsyncClient.

title: MCP-as-files provider (FileMcpConfigStore/FileMcpServerProvider, ~459 LOC) wired nowhere
detail: Exported from capabilities/mcp/__init__.py:41-42 but no production module constructs it — runtime_worker/dependencies.py:282-308 composes only BackendMcpProvider + the browser provider. Sole consumer is tests/unit/agent_runtime/capabilities/mcp/test_files.py.
suggestion: Wire it into the desktop light-file-store effort that motivated it, or delete it until that lands.

title: Hand-rolled OAuth 2.1 client and MCP JSON-RPC handshake tracked by hand in two services
detail: mcp_oauth.py (518 LOC) implements RFC 9728/8414 discovery, RFC 7591 registration, PKCE and refresh on raw urllib; BackendMcpClient (backend_provider.py:145-260) re-implements the MCP initialize/tools/list/tools/call handshake. The token-isolation architecture justifies not dropping the official mcp SDK into ai-backend, but the backend proxy could host the SDK client against the decrypted token, and authlib/httpx would replace the bespoke, blocking OAuth plumbing.
suggestion: Evaluate hosting the official mcp Python SDK client inside the backend RPC proxy and replacing mcp_oauth's transport with authlib+httpx.

title: Service-token header names re-declared by hand beside the constants package that owns them
detail: suggestible_connectors_resolver.py:33-38 declares _Headers.SERVICE_TOKEN/ORG/USER as string literals although copilot_service_contracts.headers is the SSOT and is imported for exactly these values in backend_provider.py:10-15. The same file also re-implements BACKEND_BASE_URL/fallback env resolution that runtime_worker settings own.
suggestion: Import SERVICE_TOKEN_HEADER/ORG_HEADER/USER_HEADER from copilot_service_contracts and reuse the worker settings' backend URL resolution.

title: Composer Tools listing bypasses the effective-auth-state upgrade used everywhere else
detail: ToolCatalogService.list_tools filters on the raw stored record.auth_state == AUTHENTICATED (service.py:1233-1242) while /v1/mcp/servers and /internal/v1/mcp/cards route through _effective_auth_state (usable vault token upgrades stale state, service.py:677-693). A server with a valid token but un-flipped row shows as connected in the popover but is missing from the composer Tools popover; AUTH_SKIPPED servers are likewise excluded only there.
suggestion: Route list_tools through _effective_auth_state (and decide AUTH_SKIPPED policy explicitly).

title: Desktop OAuth loopback path + deep-link URI duplicated across TS and Python, undocumented
detail: "/connectors/oauth/cb" and "enterprise://oauth/callback" are declared in packages/api-types/src/connectors-desktop.ts:37-40 and again as _LOOPBACK_PATH/_DEEP_LINK_URI in services/backend/src/backend_app/connectors/oauth_coordinator.py:53-54. Unlike the SIWE template duplication, no change-both-together note exists.
suggestion: Add the byte-identical-duplication warning both places (matching the SIWE precedent) or a conformance test.

title: N+1 token reads on every MCP card listing
detail: list_servers and list_internal_cards call _effective_auth_state -> store.get_token per server (service.py:558-597, 671-693) — one extra store query per connector on every popover open, cards fetch, and run-start against the Postgres store.
suggestion: Batch token lookups or maintain a token-state column updated on put_token.

title: Packaged DMG/NSIS builds drop the staged web assets — wallet sign-in 404s in that channel
detail: stage.mjs stages wallet.html to <dest>/web and runtime-paths.ts expects <base>/web, but electron-builder.yml extraResources maps only resources/runtime into resourcesPath — resources/web never ships. The supervisor still sets FACADE_WEB_DIST_DIR to the missing dir; the facade logs 'wallet page not served' and wallet-login.ts opens {facade}/wallet.html → 404. CLI installs are unaffected (stage writes ~/.0xcopilot/web). Fix: add a second extraResources mapping from resources/web to web.
suggestion: Add `- from: resources/web / to: web` to electron-builder extraResources and cover it with a packaged-layout assertion test.

title: Supervised ai-backend env omits BACKEND_BASE_URL — runtime resolvers fall back to Null on desktop (fixed on main post-base)
detail: buildServiceEnv gives ai-backend only MCP_BACKEND_REGISTRY_URL/SKILLS_BACKEND_REGISTRY_URL, but project_resolver/membership/notifications/routine_backend_client key off BACKEND_BASE_URL (+ENTERPRISE_SERVICE_TOKEN) and return Null implementations when unset, breaking BYOK policy resolution in supervised desktop boots at this audit base (c0efb038). main's bcc65dbb (#114) fixes the supervisor; verify run-local.mjs got the same env addition when rebasing.
suggestion: Confirm bcc65dbb covers both service-env.ts and run-local.mjs, and add a supervised-boot assertion that the ai-backend health/config reports a non-Null backend client.

title: The desktop boot is implemented twice (run-local.mjs vs supervisor) and the copies have materially drifted
detail: run-local.mjs re-implements port allocation, health polling, the psycopg create-database snippet, and the full service env table in Node. Drift is real: initdb -U postgres -A trust vs -U atlas --auth=scram-sha-256; DB names backend/ai_backend vs atlas_backend/atlas_ai; run-local sets RUNTIME_ENABLE_LOCAL_MODELS (supervisor doesn't); supervisor sets SIWE_ORIGIN/FACADE_WEB_DIST_DIR/RUNTIME_EVENT_BUS_BACKEND (run-local doesn't). Both headline claims of exact equivalence (run-local.mjs header, tools/desktop-runtime/README.md) are false, weakening every 'proven by run-local.mjs' safety comment in the supervisor modules.
suggestion: Extract one shared boot-contract source (env table + init parameters, e.g. a JSON/mjs module both consume) or drive run-local from the compiled supervisor; fix the equivalence claims in both READMEs meanwhile.

title: RUNTIME_ENABLE_LOCAL_MODELS documented as part of the desktop boot contract but never set by the real supervisor
detail: tools/desktop-runtime/README.md line 70 documents the flag as surfacing Settings → Local models and run-local.mjs sets it, but service-env.ts builds the ai-backend env without it — shipped desktop boots (CLI and packaged) do not enable the local-models section the docs promise.
suggestion: Set RUNTIME_ENABLE_LOCAL_MODELS=true in buildServiceEnv for ai-backend (same 'this is the user's machine' rationale) or correct the boot-contract doc.

title: Documented dev supervised recipe boots a posture whose default 'Sign in' button always fails
detail: COPILOT_RUNTIME_DIR=… npm run dev (root CLAUDE.md and apps/desktop/README.md) supervises with children at *_ENVIRONMENT=production (no /v1/dev/identity/mint), but without COPILOT_PRODUCTION=1 the app resolves DEV posture, so the local sign-in routes to dev-mint and 404s against its own stack; only wallet/Google work. The CLI path is unaffected.
suggestion: Fold shouldSupervise into the posture decision (a supervised production-env stack implies production posture unless COPILOT_DEV=1), or document that supervised dev requires COPILOT_PRODUCTION=1.

title: App identity, platform keys, service dir tables, and app version hand-synced across 5+ files
detail: com.0x-copilot.app appears in branding.ts, electron-builder.yml, and mac-shell.mjs; the app name in branding.ts, electron-builder.yml, and cli/paths.mjs ('must stay in sync with app.setName'); SUPPORTED_KEYS in cli/paths.mjs duplicates manifest.json's platform maps; assemble-payload.mjs mirrors stage.mjs's SERVICES table by hand; renderer APP_VERSION '0.1.0' mirrors package.json. Each pair relies on a keep-in-sync comment.
suggestion: Create one identity/constants source (JSON consumable from mjs, TS, and electron-builder via a config-generation step); derive SUPPORTED_KEYS from manifest.json; inject the app version at build time.

title: End users run Electron 42 while dev/CI/packaging exercise Electron 43
detail: The published CLI pins electron 42.1.0 and payload mode resolves the CLI's own electron first, while apps/desktop develops, unit-tests, and packages against electron 43.1.1 — the runtime real npm installs use is the one no test path covers.
suggestion: Pin both to the same version (single-source it, e.g. CLI reads the desktop app's pin at assemble time) and smoke the CLI launch in CI on that exact Electron.

title: needsStage exported from tools/cli/lib/stage.mjs with no caller
detail: needsStage(version) duplicates the staleness check that stageRuntime already performs inline; nothing in tools/cli imports it and the package has no tests.
suggestion: Delete it, or use it in doctor.mjs to report 'staged for CLI vX, current vY'.

title: Boot-phase copy duplicated between supervisor and BootProgress step log
detail: The supervisor's phase messages ('Unlocking secure storage…' etc.) live in supervisor.ts and are re-listed verbatim as the renderer's PHASES step-log labels in BootProgress.tsx; a wording change needs two edits plus the PHASE_PERCENT table.
suggestion: Move the phase list (id + label + percent) into chat-transport next to BootStatusPayloadSchema so both sides consume one table.

title: Two different default-workspace fallbacks across the IPC boundary
detail: Renderer uses DEFAULT_WORKSPACE_ID 'org_acme' (SignInGate.tsx) while main's bearer-refresh decorator falls back to COPILOT_WORKSPACE_ID ?? 'wsp_unknown' (index.ts) — audit rows can record wsp_unknown for a session the renderer calls org_acme.
suggestion: Single-source the default workspace id (chat-transport constant) and use it on both sides.

title: Six drifted _*-stub.ts contract copies + their dead legacy-route island were never rewired to api-types
detail: Each stub is headed "TODO(merge): delete this file" but the rewiring never happened. Confirmed material drift: _todos-stub.ts models `done: boolean` while the server (todos/store.py:71) and canonical api-types/todos.ts:114 use `status` + parent_id/recurrence; TodosRoute.tsx:82 reads `t.done` (always undefined) and todosApi.ts:211 sends `filter[done]` the backend never parses (routes.py:165 reads filter[status]). _home-stub's AgentActivityKind shares zero values with canonical HomeActivityKind. The entire consuming island ({Home,Todos,Inbox,Library,Routines,Agents}Route + api modules) has no importer outside itself — routes.ts:19-21 folded those destinations — so this is simultaneously the largest contract-drift instance and a large dead-code cluster (~6 routes + 6 api modules + 6 stubs).
suggestion: Delete the dead island (6 Route components, 6 api modules, 6 stubs) in one PR, or if any destination is to be revived, remount it on api-types shapes; add an eslint ban on importing _*-stub.

title: SIWE EIP-4361 byte-exact template hand-synced in THREE places; docs claim two
detail: The backend re-parses the signed text and rejects any byte drift. Copies: backend_app/identity/siwe.py:291, apps/frontend/src/features/auth/siweMessage.ts:31, and an undocumented third in apps/desktop/main/auth/local-login.ts:79. Root CLAUDE.md documents only the first two as "duplicated byte-identically ... change both together", so a template edit following the docs bricks desktop wallet login.
suggestion: Immediately: document the third copy and add one cross-copy byte-fixture test all three suites share. Better: have POST /v1/auth/siwe/nonce return the template/fields so clients render rather than re-author it.

title: At least 6 divergent hardcoded model catalogs across services and UI layers
detail: Model id/label lists are independently hardcoded in agent_runtime/api/model_catalog.py:46-122, apps/desktop/renderer/composer/desktopModelCatalog.ts:32-42, packages/chat-surface/src/composer/ModelPicker.tsx:35, apps/frontend/src/features/chat/ChatScreen.tsx:2561-2574, backend agents/service.py:66 (default anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-7-1m), and chat-surface/settings/data/providerKeys.ts:63-91 — with mutually inconsistent id schemes (gpt-5.4-mini vs anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 vs claude-sonnet-4-6). Corroborates the first-run-review finding; the ai-backend already serves a model-catalog endpoint the UI lists bypass.
suggestion: Serve one catalog from the ai-backend (the decided models.dev direction) and make every picker consume ModelCatalogResponse; delete the hardcoded lists.

title: 9,231-LOC hand-written TS wire mirror with zero automated drift detection
detail: api-types is declared server-mirroring but is fully hand-maintained (SPEC.md:76 confirms no generator); CI only runs tsc (ci-frontend.yml:49). A scripted diff shows the core enums are in sync today (RuntimeApiEventType 44/44, AgentRunStatus 8/8, RuntimeActivityKind 11/11), so the same-PR convention is holding — but only by review habit, and the stub island proves what happens when it lapses. Root CLAUDE.md's "generated contracts (packages/api-types)" misstates the safety net.
suggestion: Add a CI check that exports enum values + envelope field names from the services' openapi.json and diffs them against api-types' as-const tuples; fix the CLAUDE.md wording.

title: MCP enum set and card shape triplicated (backend, ai-backend internal mirror, api-types)
detail: McpTransport/McpAuthMode/McpAuthState/McpServerHealth are defined as StrEnums in backend_app/contracts.py:148-175, re-defined in ai-backend agent_runtime/capabilities/mcp/cards.py:45-79 for the /internal/v1 card feed (plus a McpServerCard mirror at :119), and mirrored again as TS unions in api-types/index.ts:16-28. The internal service-to-service contract has no shared constants despite service-contracts existing for exactly this purpose.
suggestion: Move the MCP enum string values into copilot_service_contracts (constants-only) and have both Python services build their StrEnums from them; TS mirror stays but gains a drift test.

title: ~200-LOC deployment-profile loader re-implemented identically in all three Python services
detail: backend/deployment_profile.py (196 LOC), backend-facade/deployment_profile.py (230 LOC, self-documenting "The same module exists (with identical surface) in each of the three Python services"), and ai-backend agent_runtime/deployment/profile.py (215 LOC) each derive the same profile→toggles table from shared constants. The derivation table is a fact, not behavior — it could ship as data in service-contracts (like adapter_allowlist.json) with thin per-service loaders.
suggestion: Publish the profile→toggles mapping as JSON in service-contracts; keep only env parsing + Pydantic freezing per service (~40 LOC each).

title: Desktop→services env-name contract is unshared string literals; already caused one shipped breakage
detail: service-env.ts:154-237 hardcodes ~20 env names (ENTERPRISE_*, RUNTIME_*, MCP_*, SIWE_ORIGIN, BACKEND_URL...) that must match each Python service's settings loader; service-contracts holds the Python-side names but is unreachable from TS. The BACKEND_BASE_URL omission that broke BYOK runs end-to-end (fixed in bcc65dbb) is this contract failing with no lint or test able to catch it.
suggestion: Add an env-name manifest JSON to service-contracts (consumable from TS exactly like adapter_allowlist.json) enumerating each service's required/optional vars; assert coverage in a desktop unit test.

title: Error contracts are prose: FE regexes backend error messages instead of typed codes
detail: http.ts:47-70 shape-sniffs FastAPI {detail}; mcpErrors.ts:11-12 classifies "OAuth setup required" by regexing the human-readable message (SETUP_PATTERN matching four phrasings). A backend copy-edit silently breaks the Setup-required CTA. The backend already has a structured ErrorResponse with a details object (runtime_api/schemas/errors.py:22) but no machine-readable code field crosses the wire for these paths.
suggestion: Add a stable `code` field to the MCP auth/install error responses and switch mcpErrors.ts to code equality; keep the regex only as fallback for old servers.

title: WindowBridge IPC interface declared twice with different channel typing
detail: apps/desktop/preload/window-bridge-types.ts:5-9 declares WindowBridge with untyped `channel: string`, while the canonical packages/chat-transport/src/ipc/window-bridge.ts:13-18 types channels as ChannelName. The preload should import the canonical interface; also rpc-protocol.ts:5's "there is no other source" comment is stale now that bridge.ts:16-22 composes two app-local channel sets.
suggestion: Delete window-bridge-types.ts in favor of the chat-transport declaration (widening to string only at the compose point) and update the rpc-protocol comment.

title: Docs-vs-code: audit-chain is Python-only (docs say "shared Python + TS") and api-types is not "generated"
detail: packages/audit-chain contains 5 Python files and no TS despite root CLAUDE.md describing it as "tamper-evident audit-chain primitives (shared Python + TS)"; the same file calls api-types "generated contracts". Both statements mislead about which safety nets exist. (The audit-chain consolidation itself is exemplary — legacy deleted, byte-compat fixtures pin history.)
suggestion: Correct both CLAUDE.md claims; if a TS audit-chain verifier is planned, track it explicitly instead of implying it exists.

title: Backend product destinations are in-memory only; schema.sql applied by nothing
detail: todos, memory, library, inbox, palette, routines, webhooks, agents, tools, connectors, projects, team have only InMemory* store implementations and create_app defaults to them (app.py:1555-1856). Each ships a schema.sql that no migration runner or code path reads. All product data AND each module's audit records vanish on backend restart — including the packaged desktop, which supervises this same backend. 0032_todos.sql even creates real tables no store uses. Docstrings claim a 'deployment-injected Postgres adapter' that does not exist in-repo.
suggestion: Write Postgres adapters for the destinations that matter (their schema.sql is already drafted — promote each into a numbered migration), and delete or clearly flag the rest as ephemeral.

title: 10 of 19 initial runtime tables have no writer anywhere
detail: runtime_checkpoints, runtime_context_payloads, runtime_compression_events, runtime_capability_snapshots, runtime_async_tasks, runtime_subagent_results, runtime_memory_items, runtime_memory_scopes, runtime_tool_invocations, runtime_legal_holds are created by migration 0001 but have zero INSERT statements in services/ai-backend/src (verified per-table). The retention sweeper sweeps three always-empty tables and migration 0011 planned encrypted columns for five of them. The live data model converged on runtime_events as the log (subagent_store folds state from events).
suggestion: Drop the dead tables in a migration (and their sweep/encryption references) or implement the writers; today the schema materially misrepresents what is persisted, which will mislead compliance reviews.

title: Privacy retention_days user setting is a dead end — resolver hook has zero callers
detail: RetentionPolicyResolver.privacy_user_retention_days (policy_resolver.py:59,89) is never passed at any of the seven construction sites (sweeper, backfill, retention routes, conversation coordinator, workspace defaults, postgres store). Three docstrings claim the wiring exists ('The retention sweeper bakes this into the existing C8 retention pipeline'). A user's 'auto-delete after N days' stores a row and renders in the UI but triggers no deletion.
suggestion: Fetch per-user privacy overrides in RetentionSweeperLoop.sweep_once (the /internal/v1/policies/runtime route already serves them) and pass privacy_user_retention_days to the resolver, or remove the setting.

title: Legal hold is checked everywhere and settable nowhere
detail: runtime_legal_holds is SELECTed by delete_user_history and sweep paths, and the file store honors conversation metadata['legal_hold'] — but no route, service, job or script in any service ever inserts a hold row or sets the flag (repo-wide grep: zero writers). The compliance-marketed control is unreachable except by manual SQL. delete_user_history also carries an acknowledged TOCTOU on the hold check.
suggestion: Add an admin legal-hold API (create/release, audited, scope org/user/conversation) or stop advertising the control; add the missing legal-hold tests CLAUDE.md requires.

title: Postgres deployments keep LangGraph checkpoints in InMemorySaver
detail: runtime_checkpointer returns the durable AsyncSqliteSaver only when RUNTIME_STORE_BACKEND=file; 'every other deployment (postgres, in-memory, web) keeps the process-local InMemorySaver' (deep_agent_builder.py:301-310). Combined with the never-written runtime_checkpoints table, the production-style shared-store path has durable events but volatile graph state — approval/graph continuation does not survive a worker restart.
suggestion: Adopt langgraph's PostgresSaver (maintained library — bespoke-replaceable gap) keyed to the existing runtime DB, and drop or repurpose runtime_checkpoints.

title: Desktop physical purge does not cascade to LangGraph checkpoints
detail: SessionEraser removes session directories and GC collects unreferenced blobs, but index/checkpoints.sqlite3 is outside the deletion plan and nothing calls the checkpointer's delete_thread (repo-wide grep: zero hits). Checkpoint channel values contain full conversation state, so the 'delete my data literally' promise (module docstring) leaves message content on disk after a purge.
suggestion: Call checkpointer.adelete_thread(conversation_id) inside the file store's _purge_conversations, and include checkpoint rows in the deletion evidence tally.

title: identity_audit_events is the only unchained, untriggered audit stream
detail: mcp/skill/runtime audit tables get HMAC chain columns, the append-only audit_writer role, and BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE triggers (backend 0002, ai 0003). identity_audit_events — logins, role grants, SCIM — is 'append-only at the repo layer' only: no seq/prev_hash/signature and no immutability trigger. audit_reader.py and api-types both encode this asymmetry (nullable chain fields). The most security-sensitive stream has the weakest tamper evidence.
suggestion: Extend the 0002 pattern (chain columns + trigger + audit_writer grants) to identity_audit_events and deploy_audit_events with a migration that flags pre-migration rows as unverifiable.

title: No retention path for outbox rows, approvals, or tool invocations
detail: RetentionKind covers only messages/events/context_payloads/checkpoints/memory_items. runtime_outbox_events has no DELETE anywhere in ai-backend src (grows unbounded); runtime_approval_requests rows are status-expired by approval_expiry_sweeper but never deleted. CLAUDE.md's own compliance checklist names outbox rows, approvals and tool invocations as retention-verification targets.
suggestion: Add outbox pruning behind the consumer-cursor low-water mark and add approvals (and, if ever written, tool invocations) as sweepable retention kinds.

title: Schema maintained in two places and already drifting
detail: Applied truth is migrations/; the 12 per-module schema.sql files are hand-maintained mirrors with no applier, and cross-references are stale — todos/store.py cites '0033_todo_series.sql' while the real migrations/0033 is tenant_settings. The legacy shims (backend_app/migrations.py, agent_runtime persistence/schema/postgres.py) re-read canonical files, but the module schema.sql set has no such guard.
suggestion: When each Postgres store lands (finding 1), promote its schema.sql into a numbered migration and delete the mirror; until then rename them *.schema.design.sql or add a CI check tying them to migrations.

title: yoyo MigrationRunner duplicated nearly line-for-line across both services
detail: services/backend/src/backend_app/db/migrate.py and services/ai-backend/src/agent_runtime/persistence/schema/migrate.py implement the same MigrationRunner (apply/rollback/status under backend.lock), identical MANIFEST.lock parse/verify and render_manifest helpers, differing only in paths and env-var names. packages/audit-chain already establishes the extraction pattern for exactly this kind of cross-cutting primitive.
suggestion: Extract a copilot-migrations shared package (runner + manifest verify + render_manifest), parameterized by migrations dir and service name, mirroring how copilot_audit_chain replaced its in-tree duplicates.
